tricking_bible.name: Pop 360 Wheel Kick
tricking_bible.prerequisites_text: spin crescent kick & jump front kick

trickipedia.name: Rodeo Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: A corkscrew where the swinging leg is grabbed by the same-side arm in a rodeo position

trickipedia.name: Slant Gainer Triple Flash Hook
trickipedia.description:
A slant gainer triple flash with a hook kick at the end.

The performer enters the trick like a slant gainer, performs 2 switch kicks after the initial swinging leg comes up and finishes with a hook or axe kick.

Can also be viewed as a Gainer Switch that turns into a Holy Hook.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Helicoptero
trickipedia.description: A butterfly kick with a helicoptero/scissorswipe at the end.  Similar to a dragonfly twist and shslugken twist combined, however the move is executed without any twisting of the upper body. The rotation must be completed by the momentum and direction of the legs alone.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Switch Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: The performer initiates a butterfly kick, switches legs, switches legs AGAIN, and then executes a hook kick upon landing.\r\n\r\nMust have high butterfly kicks and solid butterfly switch's in order to land this trick.

trickipedia.name: Swingthru 1260
trickipedia.description: Using a swing takeoff, the performer jumps and executes a 720° spin before landing with an inside kick such as a roundhouse or inside crescent kick. 180° additional spin may be added after the kick (post landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Sailor Moon Twist
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 720 Twist executed from a Sailor Moon set-up. A Sailor Moon followed by a full twist before landing.

trickipedia.name: Full Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: A full twisting flip, followed by another 90°-180° of rotation and a hook kick to finish.

trickipedia.name: Ironman
trickipedia.description: A full 360Â° followed by a backflip with your hands at your side (similar to the position Iron-man makes while flying) all in one move.\r\nNote: If hands are not by side it is a full-up-back-out

trickipedia.name: Hyper Moonkick
trickipedia.description: A moonkick/slant gainer that is hypered to land on the non-swingthru leg usually by over rotation instead of switching legs as you would with a butterfly knife or double flash kick.

trickipedia.name: Shaolin Cross Knife
trickipedia.description: This move is a Shaolin cross with an additional hook kick on the end before landing.

trickipedia.name: Hyperswipe Hook
trickipedia.description: An Aerial Twist with Sideswipe, and a Hook Kick prior to landing. Must be able to finish Hyperswipe fast and early in order to give room to execute Hook Kick.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: Execute a sideswipe followed by a gyro.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1080
trickipedia.description: a 1080 Hook Kick executed from a cheat or a tornado set up. 180 degrees of rotation begins on the floor hence the 'Cheat' name. Must have strong Cheat 720's and Cheat 900's before training this technique.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900 Double
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 900 executed with two Inner Crescent Kicks or two Roundkicks. Must be able to execute 540's upright, with enough height, and enough speed on the first kick in order to give room to complete 2nd kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Feilong
trickipedia.description: performer begins with a cheat takeoff, spinning 540degrees before a hypered outward kick (such as a hook or outside crescent) and lands with a non-hypered inside kick (such as a round or inside crescent kick) completing the total of 720 in air spin. An additional 180 may be added after the second kick (post-landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Twist Doubleleg
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 720 Twist where the legs meet at the peak of the 2nd rotation and the body folds into a pike position like a Doubleleg or a Parafuso. Must be able to complete Cheat 720 Twist fast, early, and with enough height to give room for Doubleleg finish.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900 Hyper
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 900 where practitioner would complete kick all the way thru and land on the kicking leg. Must have strong 900's with strong follow-thru on Inner Crescent or Roundkick.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Pop 900 Double
trickipedia.description: a Pop 360 followed by an Inner Crescent or Roundkick. Must keep posture up right, and have a strong follow-thru with the first kick in order to get around to the 2nd kick.

trickipedia.name: Full-In Back-Out
trickipedia.description: A fulltwist with an extra backflip afterwards

trickipedia.name: Pop 1080 Jacknife
trickipedia.description: a Pop 900 followed by a Hook kick prior to landing. Should have strong control over Hyper 900's and Jacknives before attempting this tricking technique.

trickipedia.name: Terada Grab Full-out
trickipedia.description: A Terada grab with a full twist exit.

trickipedia.name: Full-Up Terada Grab
trickipedia.description: Performer executs a layed out back somersault with 320 degrees of rotation. As they finish the rotation they grab their leading leg by their head for a split second before throwing an axe kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Reverse Hyperswipe
trickipedia.description:
trickster walks/runs/hops backwards, swings non takeoff leg behind them, executes an inverted 360 degrees twist, and then lands onto the non takeoff leg.


(ie: A Loser set up into an Aerialtwist, complete with a defined Sideswipe. Must be able to finish Aerialtwist fast and early in order to get around to the Sideswipe)

trickipedia.name: Corkscrew Doubleleg Hook
trickipedia.description: A Corkscrew variation where practitioner would execute a Corkscrew, bring their legs together in a pike position like a Doubleleg or Parafuso, and follow with a Hook Kick prior to landing.

trickipedia.name: Crowd Awakener Level 2 Hook
trickipedia.description: A Corkscrew variation where practitioner would execute a Corkscrew, an inverted Splitkick at the peak, and a Hook Kick on the way down prior to landing.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg X-out Twist
trickipedia.description: A Bioflip with an Xout at the peak, and a fulltwist on the way down prior to landing. Must be able to execute the Split at the peak in order to give enough room to twist on the way down.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg Twist
trickipedia.description: A Doubleleg with a fulltwist prior to landing. Also known as a Doubleleg Gyro. Must be able to complete Doubleleg at the height of rotation to give room for the twist on the way down.

trickipedia.name: Singleleg Twist
trickipedia.description: A Singleleg with a Fulltwist on the way down. Also known as a Singleleg Gyro. Similar to a Doubleleg Twist, but with a tucked trail leg on the first kick. Must complete Singleleg high at the peak of rotation to give enough room for 2nd rotation.

trickipedia.name: Full Shuriken Twist Round
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations\r\n\r\nA double full with a shslugken kick in the first twist and a round at the end of the second twist

trickipedia.name: Double Back Flip
trickipedia.description: A trick where the performer jumps up and rotates 720 degrees backwards along the sagittal plane before returning to his feet.

trickipedia.name: Double Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: A slant gainer with two twists before landing.

trickipedia.name: Pop 900 Hyper
trickipedia.description: Using a standard pop takeoff, the performer jumps into the air and executes 720 degrees rotation and lands while executing a hypered inside or roundhouse kick. A strong pop 900 and a strong 540 kick are required for this move.

trickipedia.name: Flash Full Double Leg
trickipedia.description: A flash full (or kick full) where a dleg is executed in the full twist

trickipedia.name: Pop Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: Pop Snapuswipe

trickipedia.name: Hyper Jacknife
trickipedia.description: A jacknife landed on the outside kicking leg

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900 Parafuso
trickipedia.description: A cheat 900 with mirrored inside and outside kicks landed on two feet. A parafuso with a 360 degree spin beforehand.

trickipedia.name: Bergen Twist
trickipedia.description: A touch down snapuswipe OR a sailor moon snapuswipe.

trickipedia.name: Cheat Parafuso 900 Double
trickipedia.description: A cheat 900 double where the first kick is a parafuso as opposed to an inside kick.

trickipedia.name: Double Full Round
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations. \r\n\r\nA double full with a round kick executed before landing.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Kickflip
trickipedia.description: After the Butterfly kick is fully completed, a Frontflip is executed before the landing.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Double Full Double Leg
trickipedia.description: A Double twisting single flip with a double leg at the end.

trickipedia.name: Full Double-leg Twist
trickipedia.description: one of the many double full variations\r\n\r\nA double full with a dleg executed in the middle of the 2 rotations.  The tricker does a 360 degree spin up then dlegs over with the flip then spins 360 degrees out of that

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: A hypered Shslugken Btwist with a hook on the end. This clip is of bubba.

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Cheat 720 Twist
trickipedia.description: A cheat 720 twist with a defined hypered outside kick upon landing.

trickipedia.name: Full Feilong Twist
trickipedia.description: one of the many double full variations\r\n\r\nThe trickster does a double full but executes a feilong in the middle of the 2 spins

trickipedia.name: Bullet Flip
trickipedia.description: Bullet Flip

trickipedia.name: Double Corkscrew Round
trickipedia.description: A double corkscrew with a round kick at the end

trickipedia.name: Backside 1260 Feilong
trickipedia.description: A backside 1260 with a crescent before the round.

trickipedia.name: Feilong Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A feilong with an extra 360 degrees of rotation at the end.  A 540 gyro with an outside crescent before the round kick.

trickipedia.name: 540 Gyro Hyper
trickipedia.description: A 540 gyro that lands on the kicking leg.\r\n\r\nLike a Hyper Snapuswipe however a 540 Gyro is done with the chest staying upright.

trickipedia.name: Backside 1260 Crowd Awakener
trickipedia.description: A backside 1260 where a crowd awakener split kick is executed instead of a round.

trickipedia.name: Pop 540 Double Gyro
trickipedia.description: A Pop 540, followed by a 540°-720° rotation to land.

trickipedia.name: Jack 9
trickipedia.description: A Jack 9 is a Jackknife combined with a cheat 900.

trickipedia.name: Pocket 9
trickipedia.description: The Pocket 900 is a double sweeping kick that uses the momentum of the Front Sweep to lift the performer into the air, and lands with a consecutive air-born round kick that turns into a front sweep. It's a Swing 900 where the skip is replaced by a Front Sweep.

trickipedia.name: Back-in Rocketboii-out
trickipedia.description: A double backflip where the second backflip is executed in rocketboii position.

trickipedia.name: Pop 1440
trickipedia.description: the performer jumps into the air and rotates 1440 degrees while executing an outside spin kick on target. The outside spin kick should be executed on the last rotation.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: A combination of the cheat 720 twist and the hyperhook. You jump and set like a 540 and complete 2 rotations before landing. Instead of landing on the leg that you pump up initially on take-off like a normal cheat 720 twist, you land on the opposite leg.  The initial pumping leg is now used to execute a hook kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Full-Doubleg-In Back-Out
trickipedia.description: A fulltwist doubleleg with an extra backflip afterwards.

trickipedia.name: Cork Shuriken Hyper Hook
trickipedia.description: A shslugken cork that hypers (lands on non-kicking leg) plus a hook

trickipedia.name: Full Swipeknife
trickipedia.description:
A Swipeknife from a Fulltwist Round.

A Fulltwist Round that has been hypered, thus having a swipe-like effect that occurs overhead, before an outside spin kick is launched out of it.

This trick is assumed to be done from standing but can be done from cartwheel or round-off

trickipedia.name: Double Butterfly Twist Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: A Hyper 720 Butterfly Twist with a Hook Kick prior to landing. prerequisite_ids: 720 Hyper Butterfly Twist\r\n

trickipedia.name: Double Corkscrew Double Leg
trickipedia.description: Double Corkscrew Double Leg

trickipedia.name: Double Corkscrew Hyper
trickipedia.description: A double corkscrew that is slightly over rotated to land on the leg that was swung up to initiate the move.

trickipedia.name: Full Double Leg Hook
trickipedia.description: A full twist followed by double leg, hook.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Double Full
trickipedia.description: A double gainerfull is a backflip off of one foot with a 720° twist in it. Unlike a double cork, a double gainerfull must be completely inverted.

trickipedia.name: Double Aerial Twist
trickipedia.description: The performer takes off into an aerial, spins 720 degrees and then lands. \r\n\r\nThere are often arguments over whether a double aerial twist is 540 degrees or 720 degrees of rotation.

trickipedia.name: Suicide Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A full inverted Snapuswipe.

trickipedia.name: Envergado Gyro
trickipedia.description: An envergado with an extra 360 degrees of rotation

trickipedia.name: Full X-Out Full
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations.\r\n\r\n  After the first 360 degrees of twisting the rotation is stalled and an X-Out is performed.  The legs then pull back in and across to complete another full 360 degrees of rotation before landing.

trickipedia.name: Double Butterfly Twist Hyper
trickipedia.description: A Butterfly Twist with 720 degrees of rotation landing on the Hyper (2nd) leg. Must be EXTREMELY efficient in order to complete enough rotation to execute the hyper.

trickipedia.name: Double Arabian
trickipedia.description: A half twist to a double frontflip

trickipedia.name: Backside 720 Double Leg Twist
trickipedia.description: Performer takes off two feet, spins 360 degrees, executes a double leg, then spins an additional 360 degrees before landing

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1080 Twist
trickipedia.description: The Cheat 1080 Twist (A California Roll) is a raiz with two twists on the end of it (ie raiz double gyro).\r\n\r\nBe carefull not to confuse the name of this move with the cheat 1080 (kick).

trickipedia.name: Triple Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: Absolutely amazing.  This trick is the next full twisting variation of a cork after double cork.  The trickster swings up like they would a cork and spins 1080º before landing.

trickipedia.name: Double Leg Double Twist
trickipedia.description: A trick in which a person jumps off of both feet, and executes a spinning outside crescent and an inside crescent at the same time followed by a 720 degrees extra spin before landing.

trickipedia.name: Backside 900 Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: The pefrormer takes off, spins 540 degrees, throws an inside crescent or round kick, and then finishes the move by spinning an additional 540 degrees before landing.

trickipedia.name: Cork Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A Corkswipe Gyro is a combination between a Corkswipe and a Snapuswipe.  The tricker takes off like a Corkscrew spins once up then swipes over and spins again before landing.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900 Double Hyper
trickipedia.description: After completing the second kick in a cheat 900 double, the trick is landed on the kicking leg.

trickipedia.name: Hyper Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A Snapuswipe (Sideswipe Gyro) with extra rotation causing you to land on the same leg that executes the kick at the beginning of the move.

trickipedia.name: Rodeo Cork Gyro
trickipedia.description: A double corkscrew where the first rotation has the rodeo cork position

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Twist Gyro
trickipedia.description: A Shslugken Twist Gyro is a Shslugken Twist where after finishing the motions of the move you execute a 360 degree extra rotation.

trickipedia.name: Pop 360 Kickflip
trickipedia.description: A 360 Crescent where, after the kick, a frontflip is performed to land. Because of the way this trick takes off, the front flip is typically slightly off axis, however as long as the hips get above the head the trick is being performed correctly.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1080 Jacknife
trickipedia.description: A cheat hyper 900 with a hook kick upon landing. A combination of a cheat hyper 900 and a cheat 1080.

trickipedia.name: Aerial Twist Double Leg Twist
trickipedia.description: An aerial twist where after the first rotation the legs are brought up into a pike position, followed by another 360 degree spin before landing.

trickipedia.name: Gyroknife
trickipedia.description: A 540 gyro hyper hook.  A tornado kick followed by 360-540 degrees of rotation before an outside kick.

trickipedia.name: Pop 900 Triple
trickipedia.description: Feilong Gyro with a round kick/inside crescent kick

trickipedia.name: Pop 720 Double Doubleleg
trickipedia.description: Pop 720 Double, where the second kick is replaced with a Doubleleg

trickipedia.name: Double Full Swipe
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations. \r\n\r\na double full where a swipe is executed at the end of the trick to hyper the double full.

trickipedia.name: Full Doubleleg Twist Round
trickipedia.description: one of the many double full variations\r\nA double full with a dleg executed in the middle of the 2 rotations. Then a round kick is completed at the end of the move just before, or as landing. The tricker does a 360 degree spin up then dlegs over with the flip then spins 360 degrees out of that

trickipedia.name: Double Butterfly Twist Round
trickipedia.description: A Butterfly Twist with 720 degrees of rotation where the practitioner throws a round kick with the non leading leg upon landing.

trickipedia.name: Double Boxcutter
trickipedia.description: A hyper double corkscrew with a hook kick prior to landing.  A boxcutter with an extra rotation before the hyperhook.

trickipedia.name: Backside 1440
trickipedia.description: 1080 degrees of rotation followed by a hook kick

trickipedia.name: Backside 1260 Hyper
trickipedia.description: A backside 1260 that lands on the kicking leg

trickipedia.name: Backside 1440 Jackknife
trickipedia.description: A backside 1440 with a round before the hook.  A backside 1260 hyperhook.

trickipedia.name: Parafuso Gyro
trickipedia.description: A parafuso followed by 360 degrees of rotation.  The parafuso equivalent of a 540 gyro.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1260
trickipedia.description: A cheat setup followed by 1080 degrees of rotation with a round kick on the last 180 degrees.

trickipedia.name: Lonestar-In Front-Out
trickipedia.description: A piked sideflip into a frontflip

trickipedia.name: Full-In Full-Out
trickipedia.description: A double twisting double backflip.\r\n\r\nThe performer flips 720° over the Sagittal plane while twisting 720° over the Transverse plane.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A butterfly twist swipe where an extra rotation is completed at the end.  A double btwist with a swipe in between the two twists.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900 Gyro
trickipedia.description: A cheat 900 followed by an extra rotation at the end.  A cheat 1260 where the round occurs at the 900 mark instead.

trickipedia.name: Double Front Flip
trickipedia.description: A front tuck with 2 flips. A 720° rotation over the transverse plane.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Feilong Hyper
trickipedia.description: The performer begins with a cheat takeoff, spinning 540 degrees before executing an outward kick (such as a hook or outside crescent) and lands with a hypered inside kick (such as a round or inside crescent kick).

trickipedia.name: Tak Full Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A Tak Full followed by a Snapuswipe.

trickipedia.name: Double Tak Full
trickipedia.description: A tak full with two twists instead of one

trickipedia.name: Double Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: A snapuswipe with two twists instead of one. Tricker must have a very high and clean snapuswipe plus a tight wrap in order to get enough rotation.

trickipedia.name: Graham Cracker
trickipedia.description: A combination of the cheat 720 twist and the hyperhook. You jump and set like a 540 and complete 2 rotations before landing. Instead of landing on the leg that you pump up initially on take-off like a normal cheat 720 twist, you land on the opposite leg.  The initial pumping leg is now used to execute a hook kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Gumbi
trickipedia.description:
The Gumbi is a transition where a cartwheel is being kicked around versus being kicked back.

Usually performed on the opposite side as a cartwheel would be, the Gumbi is entered from a Capoeira wheel kick known as "meia lua de compasso". As the leg kicks back, the hand that is free reaches behind the head and over the base hand, landing on the floor and supporting the weight of the body. The back arches hard, pushing the hips forward and creating a "slingshot" effect while landing on the kicking leg.

trickipedia.name: Triple Butterfly Twist
trickipedia.description: A butterfly twist with 3 complete spins before landing.

trickipedia.name: Quadruple Full Twist
trickipedia.description: A back layout spinning 1440 degrees before landing.

trickipedia.name: Butter Knife double
trickipedia.description:
This trick is done by executing a butter knife and then spinning once and hooking again.

Similar to a hyper hook 

-- this trick just hasnt been done!

trickipedia.name: Full Dleg Twist Round
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations. \r\n\r\na double full with a double leg in between the two spins and a round kick is executed at the end of the second twist.

trickipedia.name: Double Gainer
trickipedia.description: A double back flip off of one foot. The non take-off leg must swing from behind the practitioner.

trickipedia.name: Herald Switch
trickipedia.description: A one handed gumbi switch, very similar to a one handed cartwheel switch.

trickipedia.name: Harold Switch
trickipedia.description: A one handed gumbi switch, very similar to a one handed cartwheel switch.

trickipedia.name: Quadruple Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: A corkscrew with four full rotations. Yet to be landed.

trickipedia.name: Cork-in Back-out (Snatchcannon)
trickipedia.description: The tricker executes a corkscrew, followed by a backflip, all in one move before landing.

trickipedia.name: To Splits
trickipedia.description: To Splits

trickipedia.name: Triple Aerial Twist
trickipedia.description: A triple twisting aerial.

trickipedia.name: Rodeo Grab
trickipedia.description: Body Position where both ankles are grabbed and pulled backwards while pushing the hips forward.

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Twist
trickipedia.description:
The Shslugken Twist is a mix between the Butterfly Twist and the Illusion Twist.

Like the Butterfly Twist, the Shslugken Twist is a "true" twist, and lands one foot at a time. Like the Illusion Twist, the Shslugken Twist kicks it's base leg up and over, with the kicking foot traveling in a circle from the ground, back to the ground. However, this is not all...

Unlike the Illusion Twist, the Shslugken Twist is designed to hit on the way down, like an axe kick. Due to this, the take off is usually adjusted so that it would do exactly that.

trickipedia.name: Mega
trickipedia.description: Mega is a position/landing, typically achieved by over-rotating past the hyper position and landing on the non-hypering leg. A Hyper Aerial lands in Mega position.

trickipedia.name: Hook Kick
trickipedia.description: A Hook kick.

trickipedia.name: Helicoptero
trickipedia.description: The capoeira helicopter wheel (in portuguese: Au Helicoptero) is a cartwheel with an extra half rotation. Instead of going back down after 180°, you keep spinning your legs and land on the leg that left the floor last.\r\n\r\nThis move is incorporated into higher level tricks to make for flashy variations.

trickipedia.name: Kyro
trickipedia.description: A variation in which a trick is landed on the hook kicking leg, following a hook kick mid trick by way of over-rotation. An example is a gainer hook that spins and extra 180 degrees to land on the hooking leg.

trickipedia.name: Missleg
trickipedia.description: The act of reversing the direction of motion that a leg is traveling in order the do a trick in the new direction.\r\n\r\nin the video example watch the motions of the left leg.

trickipedia.name: Scoot
trickipedia.description: Like a roundoff, this is a joining and starting move.

trickipedia.name: Slide-In
trickipedia.description: A Slide-In

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Cork Snapuswipe
trickipedia.description: The practitioner throws a double corkscrew. In between the first and second rotation, a hook/outside crescent kick is executed followed by a roundhouse/inwards crescent kick.\r\n\r\nThis trick is the corkscrew variation of the McDirty invented by Kyle McLean.

trickipedia.name: Cartwheel
trickipedia.description: The performer hurdles forward and reaches for the ground. As the body becomes completely inverted, the legs come together. As the performer continues over to stand up from their hands, the legs snap down to the ground and the performer  brings the chest up.\r\n\r\nA Cartwheel is like a round-off but without the 180° turn.

trickipedia.name: Illusion Twist
trickipedia.description: A Pop 360 executed from the "U" dip within the "butterfly" setup. The Illusion Twist occurs when a person dips down in a U-like formation, just like in a Butterfly Twist; but instsead of wrapping across and twisting off of the base leg (like in a Butterfly Twist), you simply turn and execute a Pop 360 instead. When done correctly, it gives off the appearance of a "twisting" technique, without it actually being one.\r\n\r\nDespite being a variation of the Butterfly Twist, the Illusion Twist is actually not a twist (a horizontal spin) at all. However, the Butterfly Kick is still a prerequisite.

trickipedia.name: Scoot Hyper
trickipedia.description: A Scoot that switches legs before landing.

trickipedia.name: Full Shuriken Twist Doubleleg
trickipedia.description:
One of the many double full variations. 

This trick is done with a shslugken kick in the first twist and a dleg in the second spin, coming down for the landing

trickipedia.name: J-Step
trickipedia.description: A set-up in which the performer runs in a "J" shape to get backwards momentum into a trick. Commonly used for corks.

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Cork Jacknife
trickipedia.description: A hyper corkscrew where you kick with the non landing leg, then the landing then, then finish off with a hook kick on the end.\r\n\r\nThink of it as a mix between a corkscrew and a 720 triple

trickipedia.name: Double X-Out
trickipedia.description: A single back flipping trick with two split kick "X-Outs" in the air completed before landing.

trickipedia.name: Cat Leap
trickipedia.description: The Cat Leap (aka Cat Hang) is one of the most fundamental parkour moves for both climbing and descending.

tips: Don't jump towards an obstacle that doesn't have a ledge you can grab onto, especially at height!
step_by_step_guide.title: Jump towards the wall/obstacle, leaning back so that your feet hit first

tips: Having your feet hit first helps absorb the shock of the landing and puts you in a position where you're less likely to slip. Hands first is much more likely to slip.
step_by_step_guide.title: Grab the wall with your feet hitting the wall first or at the same time as your arms grabbing.

tips: Sinking down isn't always the best if you're going to be doing a climb-up or some other form of climb. Sinking down, however, is good for getting into a secure position on the wall.
step_by_step_guide.title: When you've grabbed the wall, sink down, extending your arms fully, letting your butt sink.
step_by_step_guide.description: you did it!

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Cork Swipenife
trickipedia.description:
Yes the move is a mouthfull, here are the motions of it:

Execute a corkskrew where at the peak of the twist you throw an outside crescent or hook kick. Then execute a swipe, and before landing throw another outside crescent or hook kick.

This move is like doing a shslugken cork into a jacknife/swipeknife.

trickipedia.name: Pike Arabian Full
trickipedia.description: An arabian flip done with the legs out straight in a "piked" position followed by another 360 degrees of spin.\r\n\r\nThe total spin should be 540 degrees.

trickipedia.name: Aerial
trickipedia.description: A cartwheel without hands. The Aerial is a trick in which the performer leans his upperbody over his base foot, and kicks up his back leg while launching off of his base foot. As the body gains lift, the leg that kicks back transfers to the other side of the body, causing the body to rotate along the coronal plane, and to make way for the landing. \r\n\r\nThe chest should face the ground just before launching and just before landing the Aerial.

trickipedia.name: X-Out Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A backflip where an X-out is done followed immediately by a flash kick all before landing.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A trick in which a person performs a Gainer with a front stretch kick coming out of it. It is essentially a Flash Kick from a Gainer setup instead of a "pop" setup.

trickipedia.name: Double Full Hyper
trickipedia.description:
One of the many double full variations. 

A double full that is slightly over rotated and landed on the "hyper leg"

if you twist left that is your right foot, if you twist right that is your left foot.

trickipedia.name: Pop 1260
trickipedia.description: This "pop" trick is performed when trickster jumps into the air and rotates 1260Â° on a transversal plane while completing an inside crescent or round kick with the opposite foot as the P10.\r\n\r\nWhen performing the Pop 1260 Crescent/Round Kick, keep in mind that only 1080Â° of rotation takes place in the air. The kick is similar to a Pop 900 Kick, but you need to complete 1 extra 360Â° spin before the kick.

trickipedia.name: Triple Full
trickipedia.description: A single flip with three full twists. In this trick, the performer jumps up and rotates 360 degrees backwards along the sagittal plane (or about the x-axis) while at the same time spinning 1080 degrees along the transverse plane, before landing.

trickipedia.name: Pop 540
trickipedia.description: Taking off both legs from a ready position, the practitioner  rotates 540° along the transverse plane while executing an inside crescent kick and landing on the kicking leg. This move is very similar to the regular 540, but with a "pop" setup instead of the "cheat" setup.

trickipedia.name: Backside 720
trickipedia.description: A 360 (one) spin with an outside kick such as a hook or outside crescent. The performer begins in a "backside" stance and uses a "pop" takeoff to begin the move. An additional 180 after kick (post landing) may be added to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720
trickipedia.description: The second "cheat" trick. The Cheat 720 is a trick where the performer executes an outside spin kick while spinning 540 degrees from the "cheat" setup.\r\n\r\nSimulating the Pop 720, the Cheat 720 still has 720 degrees of rotation from initial position to terminating position; however, only 540 degrees is performed in the air. The outside spin kick is fired off at the 360 degrees mark.

trickipedia.name: Wall Running
trickipedia.description: Running on the wall. The fundamental skill of trampwall.

The first "cheat" trick. The 540 is a trick in which the performer executes a tornado kick and lands on the kicking leg.

From a ready stance, the performer turns away from the target as if to perform an outside spin kick...instead, that action is faked, and the knee pulls upwards sharply as the performer jumps off his base leg. At this moment, a tornado kick is made ready; but instead of kicking it out like normal, the hips violently turn over and the kick is directed up and over in an arc-like fashion...leaving you to land on your kicking leg.

Due to the nature of this kick, the soonest you can return to stance is after 540° of rotation from the initiation of the trick to the terminating of the same.

trickipedia.name: Precision Jump
trickipedia.description: A precision jump, also called a pre, is  a jump where you land on a pre determined spot almost always on the balls if your feet. If the obstacle has an edge, such as a curb, you want about half of your foot on the obstacle, so that your heels are hanging off, and the same goes for landing on a rail or bar.
trickipedia.safety_notes: If you are trying to do a difficult pre into a high obstacle. Try to land in a crane landing if possible, in order to ensure you have the right spacing and distance awareness for the jump.

trickipedia.name: Double Butterfly Twist
trickipedia.description: A Butterfly Twist with 720 degrees of rotation. Practitioner should muster a solid combination of height, speed, straight lined body positioning, and tight wrap in order to perform this technique.

trickipedia.name: Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A trick in which a person performs a Backflip with a front stretch kick coming out of it.\r\n\r\nWhen doing the Flash Kick, it is important to push the hips out before you begin to kick the leg through the target. The moment the body is completely upside down, with the "flash leg" extended and pointing striaght up, and the trail leg lagging behind is the key moment in the flash, and is known as the "flash position".

trickipedia.name: Moon Kick
trickipedia.description: A Gainer-hook. A trick where a person executes a Gainer, but pitches to the side a little bit and executes an outside spin kick (usually hook or wheel) with the trailing leg.\r\n\r\nThere are generally two ways to execute this trick: 1) kicking the swinging leg straight up, or 2) kicking it around and towards the shoulder like a kicking a ball. The first method produces the type of Moonkick you see in the vid above. Doing the latter produces the "Chuta Na Lua", which is the original Moonkick.

trickipedia.name: Raiz
trickipedia.description: Commonly pronounced as "rise" or "raise", a Raiz is a Gumbi without hands. A trick where an Aerial is being kicked around versus being kicked back. The Raiz can also be depicted as a Gainer executed from the "cheat" setup.

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg
trickipedia.description:
A trick in which a person jumps off of both feet, and executes a spinning outside crescent and an inside crescent at the same time.

The Doubleleg is identical to the Feilong in terms of take off, aerial rotation, and landing. The only difference is the way both feet come up and over the body. In a Feliong, it comes up one at a time. In a Doubleleg, both legs come up at the same time, mirroring each other as they sweep through the target. 

The moment both feet are outstretched in front of the performer, in a piked position with the feet pointing straight up, is the most important moment in the Doubleleg. This single frame is known as "the doubleleg position", and must be entered and exited in the same way as the standard Doubleleg to constitute what it is to do a Doubleleg.

trickipedia.name: Backside 900
trickipedia.description: A 540 (one and one half) in air spin followed by an inward kick such as a roundhouse or inside crescent. The performer starts in "backside" stance and uses a "pop" takeoff to begin the move. An additional 180 may be added after the kick (post landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Parafuso
trickipedia.description: A "cheat" trick in which the performer executes a Doubleleg. Stemming from the tornado kick, the Parafuso occurs when the cheat leg of the tornado kicks all the way up like a spin outside crescent while jumping off of the base leg. Once the cheat leg lines up with the target, the base leg is brought up and over, slamming into the cheat leg and driving it through the target.\r\n\r\nA good Parafuso will produce a clapping sound from when the base leg slams into the cheat leg. This sound should occur at the Doubleleg position.

trickipedia.name: Illusion Twist Feilong
trickipedia.description: A Feilong executed from the "U" dip within the "butterfly" setup. The Illusion Twist Feilong occurs when a person dips down in a U-like formation, just like in a Butterfly Twist; but instsead of wrapping across and twisting off of the base leg (like in a Butterfly Twist), you simply turn and execute a Feilong instead.

trickipedia.name: Touchdown Aerial Twist
trickipedia.description: This move is executed by starting a one-handed cartwheel, and springing off the hand into a twist to finish the trick.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 900
trickipedia.description: The fourth "cheat" trick, the Cheat 900 is a manuever where the performer executes an inside spin kick while spinning 720° from the "cheat" setup.\r\n\r\nSimulating the Pop 900, the Cheat 900 still has 900° of rotation from initial position to terminating position; however, only 720° is performed in the air. The inside spin kick is fired off at the 540° mark.

trickipedia.name: Sideswipe
trickipedia.description: A Raiz Switch. An inverted 540. The Sideswipe occurs when an aggresive switch is used just before the spine aligns with the ground dslugng the execution of a Raiz. This creates a swiping motion that denotes the characteristic of any "swipe" (a hypering inside spin kick done while inverted). This trick is landed in the same fashion as as a Flash Kick.

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg X-out
trickipedia.description: The Doubleleg X-Out is a trick where a Doubleleg is being canceled for an X-Out.\r\n\r\nPerformed like any other "pop" trick, as soon as the performer leaves the ground, the  shoulders turn away and drop back as the hips are brought over. This shifts the body into a position where it can flip out of the "pop" trick, as opposed to turning out of it like normal. While the subject flips over, the legs are chambered until the knees are pointing at the ceiling. Once they do, the legs are shot outwards into the "X-Out position" (kicked high and with both legs spread to match one's own wingspan, as the arms spread outwards). Once the legs lock out completely, they are retracted back inwards before the flip completes. This trick terminates by landing on both feet at the same time.

trickipedia.name: Backside 1080
trickipedia.description: A 720 (two) spin with an outside kick upon landing such as a hook or outside crescent kick. The performer starts in a "backside" stance and uses a "pop" takeoff to begin the move. An additional 180 spin after kick (post landing) may be added to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Rodeo
trickipedia.description:
Initiated in a manner similar to the Doubleleg X-Out/Kim Do Kick.  The person punches off of two feet while turning and dropping the shoulders to allow the hips to push overhead and cause the body to flip.  As the person begins to flip they grab both ankles and pull backwards while pushing the hips forward.  The person then lets go of the ankles so they can land on both feet or in a crouched position.    

Also like the Doubleleg and X-Out, the Rodeo is a type of position in the air - characterized be the hips being pushed foward, and the feet being drawn back by the dramatic pull of the arms.

Due to this, many tricks can be altered so that it features the "rodeo" position within it. The clip here shows a "Rodeo Backflip" which is considerably easier to perform than the original Rodeo, which is from a Kim Do Kick.

trickipedia.name: Back Handspring Twist
trickipedia.description: A back handspring twist is a backwards rotation where the person plants both hands on the ground and "springs" off, to also complete a full 360 twist before landing.

trickipedia.name: Tai-Fighter
trickipedia.description: the Tai-fighter is a stylish variation to the Crescent Singleleg, can be considered a "Singleleg/Feilong" hybrid. The performer goes into Crescent Singleleg and clutches the further leg into a "figure four" position. Once the primary foot sweeps past the sky, begin to round kick the clutched further foot.

trickipedia.name: Tak 9
trickipedia.description: When you approach the end of the "cheat set-up" (while the knee is facing the target), you do a frontflip off the base leg. As you start to get upsidedown, you turn out of the frontflip and into the end of a "fulltwist round" it's one of the few tricks that rotates on all axes.

trickipedia.name: Pop 1080
trickipedia.description: A "pop" trick in which the performer jumps into the air and rotates 1080° while executing an outside spin kick on target. The outside spin kick should be executed on the last rotation.\r\n\r\nWhen executing the Pop 1080 Wheel Kick, keep in mind that only 900° of rotation takes place in the air, and that the kick should executed after breaching the 720° mark. After the kick is fired off, you should be on the ground with your kicking leg pulling your body the extra 180°, finishing the 1080° requirement.

trickipedia.name: Double Full Doubleleg Swipe
trickipedia.description: One of the many double full variations. \r\n\r\na double full that has a double leg executed towards the end of the second rotation and is over-rotated slightly to land on the "hyper" leg

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1080 Triple
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 1080 kick in which the performer takes of from the "cheat" set-up and spins twice in the air while executing three hook or wheel kicks.

trickipedia.name: Crowd Awakener Level 4
trickipedia.description: A crowd awakener out of a tak 9.\r\nie:\r\n"Cheat Arabian" + Crowd Awakener + half-twist to crouch

trickipedia.name: Full Doubleleg Twist
trickipedia.description: A fulltwist completed with the legs in a piked "double leg position" followed by a second 360 degree twist before landing.

trickipedia.name: Illusion Hyperhook
trickipedia.description:
The Illusion Hyperhook is the first "Hyperhook Double" variation, and is basically a Hyperhook that has primary kick before the the twisting begins.

This is the same move as a shiruken hyper hook.
When the exiting the dip, an "illusion twist" type kick is executed, and snapped back against the leg that initially kicks up. From here, a secondary wrap is used, and the body turns over, gyro'ing into a Hyperhook.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 1440
trickipedia.description: The Cheat 1440 is a trick in which the performer executes an outside spin kick while spinning 1080 degrees from the "cheat" setup.\r\n\r\nOfficially landed by Minhyuk Lee

trickipedia.name: Gyro
trickipedia.description: As opposed to "twist," a gyro is where a 360 degree twist is performed after some portion of the trick & brings the performer into the landing. In the example of a snapuswipe, (a sideswipe gyro) the kick in the sideswipe is performed fully and then a twist is performed while still horizontal. In a doubleleg gyro, the entire flip is done before a vertical gyro to finish the move.

trickipedia.name: in/out
trickipedia.description: Gymnastics terminology that has been adapted for tricking that describes how a move is started or ended.   It's a simplified version of describing moves  that have multiple body positions.  A simple example would be the X-out.  The performer does a backflip but finishes or "goes out" of the flip with the body in the X position.  So the performer created an "X" out of the flip.  Another more extensive example would be a double full twisting back flip with an x-out done in between the two full twists which would be called a full-in X-Out full-out.  The performer starts or "goes In" the move with a full twist and then performs and X-Out and finishes or "goes out" with a full twist.  This terminology allows one to completely describe a move in a few words rather than a few sentences.

trickipedia.name: Handstand
trickipedia.description: An inverted balance on both hands with a straight body, held for 2+ seconds. Develops upper body strength and alignment.

trickipedia.name: Balance
trickipedia.description: just a way of keeping your body stable (usually on a bar/rail)
trickipedia.tips_and_tricks: use your hips as a stabilizer

trickipedia.name: Cartwheel
trickipedia.description: A sideways rotation with hands and feet alternating on the ground, forming a star shape. Builds lateral movement and balance.

trickipedia.name: Backward Roll
trickipedia.description: A backward somersault, rolling from feet to shoulders and back to standing. Develops inversion skills and core strength.

trickipedia.name: Quadrupedal Movement
trickipedia.description: Movement on all fours. A bear crawl.

trickipedia.name: Handstand Roll
trickipedia.description: A handstand transitioning into a forward roll. Bridges handstand and rolling skills for beginners.

trickipedia.name: Dive Roll
trickipedia.description: A forward roll with a slight jump or dive forward, landing into a roll. Introduces momentum for advanced skills.

trickipedia.name: Au-Batido
trickipedia.description:
A side stretch kick executed from a handstand position.

A trick where a person kicks back one leg while putting the opposite corresponding hand on the ground. As you become inverted, you place your other hand on the ground and remove your initial base hand, kicking the leg you pushed off the ground with towards your ear. This all must be done while maintaining the handstand.

The Au-Batido is mostly characterized be the position of the perfomer when the kick makes contact, which somewhat looks like a frozen one-handed cartwheel. Due to this, there can be many ways to perform this trick.

trickipedia.name: Forward roll
trickipedia.description: Forward roll

trickipedia.name: Pop 360
trickipedia.description: The Pop 360 Wheel Kick is a trick in which the performer jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 360° while delivering an outside spin kick to the target before landing on the ground.

trickipedia.name: Forward Roll/Somersault
trickipedia.description: Forward Roll

trickipedia.name: Roundhouse kick
trickipedia.description: The roundhouse kick involves stepping overwhile snapping your leg to the side while spinning 180 degrees

trickipedia.name: Seat Drop
trickipedia.description: Drop to your butt

trickipedia.name: Webster
trickipedia.description: An aerial takeoff into a frontflip. The performer kicks the back leg up first, quickly followed by the front leg and into the forward flip.

trickipedia.name: Hyper Feilong
trickipedia.description: The Hyper Feilong is a trick in which a person jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 360° while executing an outside spin kick to the target, then delivering an inside kick to the same target before landing on the trailing leg.\r\n\r\nThe definative action of a Hyper Feilong is to perform two kicks in the specific arrangement of 'outside to inside'. The most popular arrangements are outside crescent to inside crescent, and outside crescent to front.

trickipedia.name: Safety Vault/Step Vault
trickipedia.description: The Safety Vault is a vault used in parkour that us much easier variation of the side vault, except the the outside foot is placed on top of the obstacle and the inside arm is then released, while the outside foot pushes the practitioner off the obstacle. This vault can also be called a step vault.

step_by_step_guide.title: Approach the obstacle face on
Place a hand on the obstacle and the opposite leg on the obstacle
Lift the inside leg through as the body moves over the obstacle
Land on the inside leg and continue forward running movement

trickipedia.name: Swing-Through
trickipedia.description: A popular combo  or transition technique where momentum is used from one trick to carry directly into the second with no stalling.  The swing thru requires the first trick to be one that is normally landed one foot after another.  The trailing leg or second leg that would normally land now misses the ground or "swings thru" to initiate the second trick.  Popular examples are btwist to gainer flash or Raiz to gainer flash.

trickipedia.name: Back Flip
trickipedia.description: Known as a 'Mortal' in Capoeira, the Backflip is the most commercial of any other skill. While being seemingly difficult to the average person, the Backflip is actually very elementary.\r\n\r\nOne thing about the Backflip is that there are a few variations of it; however, when using the term 'backflip', it is often assumed that it is a 'back tuck'.

trickipedia.name: Stepover Hook
trickipedia.description: The nickname given to the first swing kick. Using a swing takeoff (like a gainer), the performer jumps onto his "inside" leg, spinning 180 degrees in air and upon landing executes an outside kick such as a hook or crescent kick with the other leg. An additional 180 spin may be added after the kick (post landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Plyo
trickipedia.description: A two footed jump that is taken directly after a landing.

trickipedia.name: Kip-up
trickipedia.description: Mostly used to quickly move from a prone position to a standing one. The Kip-up is done by first lying on one's back, and bringing both legs high into the chest before kicking them upwards and away from the chest. If done correctly, this action puts some pressure on the neck and the back of the head, which creates a rebounding effect that allows the performer to get his feet under him and land standing up.\r\n\r\nThe arms and hands can be used to take pressure off the head and make the trick a little more easier to do.

trickipedia.name: Stride
trickipedia.description: A one footed jump taken directly after landing on that same foot. Very similar to stepping, but with more distance.

trickipedia.name: Auto-Bahn
trickipedia.description:
Very similar to a narabong but the round kick is never executed, it's just a false kick. 

chamber-chamber-hook

where as a narabong is:

chamber-round-hook

trickipedia.name: Crash dive
trickipedia.description: A dynamic movement where you dive towards the trampoline wall from the trampoline bed.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Kick
trickipedia.description:
A trick where the performer places his base foot further away from his body, before dipping his upperbody down and back up in a "U" figuration. While he does this, his chest transfers from being over his back leg to being over his base leg. As the performer lifts his chest back upwards, he kicks up his back leg while launching off of his base foot. The combination of these actions should propel the performer into a flatspin. Once the back leg reaches the zenith of it's kicking motion, the base leg is kicked upwards as well while the back leg drops back down. The trick terminates by landing on the foot of the back leg and returning to stance.

The Butterfly Kick is a very sensitive trick, many things must be achieved to make it legit. While the performer is airborne the arms must be held out to the side. While kicking the base leg back, the chest must be facing forward while the navel is pointing downwards. There also must be a 180° flatspin while airborne.

trickipedia.name: Pop 720
trickipedia.description: The Pop 720 Wheel Kick is a trick in which the performer jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 720° while delivering an outside spin kick to the target before landing on the ground.

trickipedia.name: Pop 360 Gyro
trickipedia.description: The Pop 360 Gyro is a trick in which the performer jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 360Â° while delivering an outside spin kick to the target, and continuing to spin an additional 180Â° before landing on the ground

trickipedia.name: Singleleg
trickipedia.description: The Singleleg is a mix between a Hyper Pop 360 and a Doubleleg.\r\n\r\nTypically performed as a Doubleleg with the inside crescent leg bent, the Singleleg does have room for stylistic impression. Since the trail leg isn't really doing anything, it can be grabbed and tweaked in various different positions.

trickipedia.name: Parkour Roll
trickipedia.description:
A safety roll that one performs to distribute the impact of from drop. A proper parkour roll can prevent injuries and allow an athlete to jump from very high distances.

Safety rolls should be used when a landing is too dangerous to attempt with simply bending the legs.

trickipedia.name: Sailor Moon
trickipedia.description: Raiz variation where the person plants one hand on the ground before take off.  The move is started in the same manner as the capoeira meia lua compasso.  Once the hand is planted on the ground the kicking leg is extended you push off the ground and finish the maneuver like a raiz.

trickipedia.name: Crowd Awakener
trickipedia.description: The Crowd Awakener is a trick where a split kick is being executed from a "cheat" setup.\r\n\r\nMore specifically, the Crowd Awakener occurs when a Parafuso is aborted midway through it's kicking process, and is turned into some kind of X-Out hybrid. The legs are in the X-Out position; with the hips high, but not overhead. The base leg must perform a twist kick or "inverted front kick" to properly get to this position.

trickipedia.name: Coin Drop
trickipedia.description: A Coin Drop is a transition made from Toprock directly into a Backspin, or more commonly into Windmills. It requires that the dancer shifts his/her weight from the left leg to the right leg, leans onto his/her right hand and then moves the weight onto his/her left hand/forearm - shoulder while his/her feet leave the ground. The momentum generated from performing a Coin Drop sends the dancer into a spin onto his/her back.

trickipedia.name: Pop Swipe
trickipedia.description: A "side-flash" either done from standing or from a 'pop' setup. A Sideswipe without the 'cheat' setup. With the back facing the target, the Pop Swipe is a trick where a person jumps off of both feet, and sways his upperbody back while pushing his hips forward. As the body becomes inverted, the trailing leg is swiped over towards the far shoulder. The trick terminates by landing on the kicking leg and returning to stance.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Swingthru 900
trickipedia.description: The second swing kick. Using a swing takeoff, the performer jumps into the air executing a 360 (one) spin and lands with an inside kick such as a roundhouse or inside crescent kick. 180 additional spin may be added after the kick (post landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Suicide Kip Up
trickipedia.description: The performer jumps backwards from standing onto their shoulders and hands (hands positioned behind shoulders) and the does a kip up back into the standing position

trickipedia.name: Barani
trickipedia.description: A roundoff with no hands where the practitioner takes off of both feet into the move

trickipedia.name: Donut Boy
trickipedia.description: A Piked vertical spinning trick.

trickipedia.name: Spyder
trickipedia.description: This trick enters the same way a raiz would, however the momentum is directed around in a butterfly kick motion instead of over like a raiz.

trickipedia.name: Slant Gainer
trickipedia.description: This move is a non-inverted gainer flash. The kick swings around the side rather than over the top. Some people thing of it as a swing-through set-up into a butterfly kick.

trickipedia.name: Valdez
trickipedia.description: A powermove similar to a backhand spring except, only with one hand
The 1990 is a trick that occurs when a person kicks or presses into a handstand position just before wrapping his legs together and spinning on one hand.

trickipedia.name: Hypertwist
trickipedia.description: A Butterfly Twist landed on the opposite leg by way of overrotating the twist.\r\n\r\nA trick in which a 540° twist is being executed while the body rotates 180° through the transverse plane (a flatspin). This trick is executed from the 'butterfly' setup, and the wrap for the twist occurs at the same time you would kick up the back leg in the Butterfly Kick. The landing should feel similar to landing a Butterfly Kick.

trickipedia.name: Envergado
trickipedia.description: The Envergado is a Doubleleg from a canceled Raiz. In other words, it\'s an inverted Parafuso.\r\n\r\nThe Envergado occurs from the Raiz. Once the back leg is kicked back and the body has become airborne, the shoulders turn outwards and the stomach is tightened, pulling both legs over in a 'piked' position. The trick is landed on both feet at the same time. The 'doubeleg position' should be entered before the spine is perpendicular to the ground.

trickipedia.name: Feilong
trickipedia.description: The Feilong is a trick in which a person jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 360° while executing an outside spin kick to the target, then delivering an inside kick to the same target before landing on the ground.\r\n\r\nThe definative action of a Feilong is to perform two kicks in the specific arrangement of "outside to inside". The most popular arrangements are outside crescent to inside crescent, and outside crescent to front.

trickipedia.name: Pop 900
trickipedia.description: Using a standard pop takeoff, the performer jumps into the air and executes 720 degrees rotation and lands while executing a non-hypered inside or roundhouse kick (This usually accounts for the other 180 degrees in the trick name).

trickipedia.name: Pop 720 Double
trickipedia.description: Using a standard pop takeoff, the performer jumps up and executes a hypered outward kick (such as the first kick in a 360 crescent kick) and continues rotation around to land with a non-hypered outward kick (as in a pop720).

trickipedia.name: Jackknife
trickipedia.description: A 540 kick which has an outside hook or wheel kick thrown upon landing. Similar to cheat720 but with a hypered inside kick before the hook. Can sometimes be done with 2 chambered kicks as long as both are visible.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Arabian
trickipedia.description: A one footed Arabian. The Gainer Arabian is a trick where the performer swings his back leg up, while jumping off of his base leg, using the force of the swinging leg to propel him in a 360° rotation about the sagittal plane while turning 180° through the transverse plane.\r\n\r\nLike the Arabian, the half turn must be completed before the hips rotate above the head.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Full
trickipedia.description: A Gainerfull is essentially a Backfull off of one foot. The Gainerfull is a trick where the performer swings his back leg up, while jumping off of his base leg, using the force of the swinging leg to propel him in a 360 degrees rotation about the sagittal plane while spinning 360 degrees through the transverse plane.\r\n\r\nA Gainer Full must be fully inverted!

trickipedia.name: Front Handspring
trickipedia.description: A frontwards rotation, where the person plants both hands on the ground and "springs" off on to his feet.

trickipedia.name: 540 Gyro
trickipedia.description: The 540 Gyro is essentially a 540 tornado kick, followed by a 180° vertical spin before landing. Unlike the traditional 540, the gyro doesn't really allow you to land on your kicking leg in the same manner, and so landing on both legs or even the non-kicking leg is fair practice.

trickipedia.name: Sailor Moon Knife
trickipedia.description: A combination of the Sailor Moon/One-Handed Raiz and Cheat 720.  The person executes a Sailor Moon but with added rotation to land on the trail leg.  The opposite leg is chambering to execute a hook kick upon landing.

trickipedia.name: Terada Grab
trickipedia.description: A quarter twisting back flip where the outer leg is grabbed while upside down and dropped like an axe kick.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Feilong Cross
trickipedia.description: The Feilong Cross is a Feilong altered so that both kicks travel across, as opposed to traveling up and over.\r\n\r\nThe Feilong Cross is a trick in which a person jumps off of both feet, from a ready position, turns 360° while executing a hook kick to the target, then delivering a roundhouse kick to the same target before landing on the ground.

trickipedia.name: Pop 360 Double
trickipedia.description:
The Pop 360 Double is a trick when a person executes a Pop 360, but kicks the outside spin kick twice without spinning around an additional time.

This trick pretty much calls for a hook kick to be used, because it's basically the only outside spin kick that can be cycled smoothly enough for this trick to look good.

trickipedia.name: Triple Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A Triple Flashkick is a flipping technique with 3 complete Flashkicks executed in the air prior to landing. prerequisite_ids: Flashkick, Double Flashkick.

trickipedia.name: X-out
trickipedia.description: A Backflip with a split-like kick executed at the zenith. While the subject flips over, the legs are chambered until the knees are pointing at the ceiling. Once they do, the legs are shot outwards into the 'X-Out position' (kicked high and with both legs spread to match one's own wingspan, as the arms spread outwards). Once the legs lock out completely, they are retracted back inwards before the flip completes. This trick terminates by landing on both feet at the same time.\r\n\r\nThe X-Out should be acheived before the head of the performer points at the ground.

trickipedia.name: Suicideswipe
trickipedia.description: A completely inverted Sideswipe.

trickipedia.name: Arashiswipe
trickipedia.description: An Arashiswipe is a move where the performer places a hand down similar to a scoot setup,but insted off swinging up to transition, the person swings there legs around similar to a gainerflash. As there hand is still on the floor the person pivots around landing where they started from.

trickipedia.name: Gainer X-out
trickipedia.description: A one footed X-Out. The Gainer X-Out is a trick where the performer swings his back leg up, while jumping off of his base leg, using the force of the swinging leg to propel him in a 360 degrees rotation about the sagittal plane. While this happens, an X-Out is performed.\r\n\r\nLike all X-Out variations, the X-Out should occur before the spine becomes perpendicular to the floor, and should rechamber inwards before landing.

A 2000 is a 1990 with both hands.\r\n\r\nWhen doing the 2000, you go about it the same way you would a 1990. The difference occurs when you get ready to wrap your legs up. Instead of wrapping and removing one hand from the floor, you wrap and move that same hand on top of your base hand.

trickipedia.name: Grandmaster Swipe
trickipedia.description: An Aerial done off of the other foot. A Masterswipe without hands.\r\n\r\nIt isn't a Grand Masterswipe unless your hips are sideways.

trickipedia.name: Sailor Moon Swipe
trickipedia.description: A sailor moon swipe. A sideswipe with the leading hand touching the ground upon take off.

trickipedia.name: Full Round
trickipedia.description: A Fulltwist Roundkick is a Fulltwisting flip with a Roundkick at the peak of the rotation. Twist must be completed early in order to extend Roundkick all the way. prerequisite_ids: Backflip and Standing Fulltwist.

trickipedia.name: Loser
trickipedia.description: A variation of the webster.  A person would take a series of short steps backwards an then perform a webster.  The name for the move was created as a joke in reference to the gainer.  A gainer is a back flip while traveling forward and a loser is the opposite, a front flip while traveling backwards.

trickipedia.name: Swingthru 1080
trickipedia.description: Using a swing takeoff, the performer jumps and executes a 540 (one and one half) spin before landing with an outside kick such as a hook or an outside crescent kick. An additional 180 spin may be added after the kick (post landing) to return to stance.

trickipedia.name: Rocketboii
trickipedia.description: Similar to the pike which is a back flip variation.  The person jumps and instead of bringing the knees in like a tuck the legs are kept straight and the feet are kicked towards the head while both arms grab behind the calves to create the pike body position.  When doing the Rocketboii variation the legs are kicked into the pike position but only one arm grabs the leg on the opposite side of the body.  The opposite arm hangs outward and the chest is slightly turned out as well.

trickipedia.name: Pop 720 Feilong
trickipedia.description: This move is a pop hyper 720 where you throw an extra round kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Swingthru Feilong
trickipedia.description: This move is the prerequisit to a shaolin cross.\r\n\r\nThe peformer runs into a swingthru, turns 180 degrees and throws an outside crescent followed by an inwards crescent or round kick. The performer should land on the leg that threw the initial outside crescent.

trickipedia.name: Spiral Knuckle
trickipedia.description: Very similar to a btwist.  There is no flat spin on this trick so the hips stay in line with where they took off from.

trickipedia.name: Swingthru Double Leg
trickipedia.description: The preformer takes off like a gainer flash but uses the momentum of the swinging leg to get height as apposed to help with rotation as a flip. \r\n\r\nThe performer takes off, brings the swinging leg together with the takeoff leg at the peak of the jump (ie double legs) and then turns their hips to the floor to land. Whilst doing the move the performer also completes 360 degrees of rotation with their chest upright.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Double Leg
trickipedia.description: The performer runs into a gainer. As they gainer they turn their chest towards the swinging leg and perform a double leg before landing.\r\n\r\nA lot of people confuse a gainer double leg with a swignthru double leg.

trickipedia.name: 360 Suicide Kip Up
trickipedia.description: The performer starts the move standing, jumps backwards and spins 360 degrees to land on their shoulders and hands (hands positioned by shoulders), they then finish the move by doing a kip up back to their feet

trickipedia.name: Pop Jacknife
trickipedia.description: A jacknife taking off of two feet as opposed to one, a combination of a pop 540 and 720. This in combination with a hyper fei-long and 720 double make up the pre-requisites for a 720 triple

trickipedia.name: Double Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A back flipping technique with two defined kicks in the air, landing on the second kicking leg. The performer should kick with the non-landing leg first, as soon as they take off, and proceed to complete the second kick.

trickipedia.name: Takuraba
trickipedia.description: At the end of the cheat setup, while the knee is still facing the target, a Frontflip is performed from the base leg.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Axe Webster
trickipedia.description:
a front flip that is executed from an ax kick.

the leg swings thru and never touches the ground in the process.

The front somersault out of it must maintain the axe kick throught. If it doesn't then it is only an axe frontflip

trickipedia.name: Au-batido 1990
trickipedia.description: An Aubatido 1990 is a 1990 handspin ending with an Aubatido or L-Kick or K-Kick.

trickipedia.name: Dragonfly Twist
trickipedia.description: Btwist Round, where the round kick is executed before the twist. After the butterfly setup is completed, re-chamber the lifting foot and execute a round kick towards your target, followed by a full gyro.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Flik Flak
trickipedia.description: Flik-Flak is like aGainer Triple Flash, but it is executed sideways (like a Corkscrew-Round without the twist)

trickipedia.name: Axe Aerial Switch
trickipedia.description: an axe kick into an aerial switch where the axe kicking foot swings behind into the aerial without touching the floor.

trickipedia.name: 540 Switch
trickipedia.description: a 540 kick where the practitioner doesn't land on the kicking leg, but instead switches landing legs just at the right moment.

trickipedia.name: Donut Boy Hook
trickipedia.description: Not sure what to make of this new move!

trickipedia.name: Spyder Twist
trickipedia.description: A Spyder with a twist. Similar to a wrap 900 but a Spyder Twist must have flatspin.

trickipedia.name: Tak Full
trickipedia.description: A cheat/vanish setup into a full.

trickipedia.name: Grandmaster Terada Swipe
trickipedia.description: After the grandmaster swipe starts, the tricker initiates a Terada grab.

trickipedia.name: Front Full
trickipedia.description: A Frontflip with a 360° twist. This is significantly harder to perform than a back full.

trickipedia.name: Full Hyper
trickipedia.description: A full twisting flip that lands on the hypering (swingthru) leg.

trickipedia.name: Pocketknife
trickipedia.description: The Pocketknife is a double sweeping kick that uses the momentum of the Front Sweep to lift the performer into the air, and lands with an air-born hook kick that turns into a rear sweep. It's a Skip Hook where the skip is replaced by a Front Sweep.

trickipedia.name: Wackknife
trickipedia.description: A wackknife is a Jackknife with only 180 degrees of rotation.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Rocketboii
trickipedia.description: A trick in which the tricker swings of one foot as if they are about to do a gainer, but then instead of maintaining the swing-through, both legs meet together while upside down in a rocketboii position

trickipedia.name: Touchdown Envergado
trickipedia.description: Like a td-raiz except lands with two feet instead of one. Commonly used for fulls or other height-needing tricks.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Flash Gyro
trickipedia.description: A gainer flash with a gyro. Swing through leg MUST maintain a swing through dslugng the gyro.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Hyper
trickipedia.description: This is a cheat 720 where you land on the kicking leg.

trickipedia.name: Wrap Full
trickipedia.description: A cartwheel fulltwist off the other leg. Most trickers will get this confused with a grandmaster twist, but the difference is that a grandmaster twist lands on the non-wrap leg as opposed to a wrap full that lands on the wrapping leg. This trick is considerably easier then a cart full because it is more closely related to an a-twist as opposed to a back full because like an a-twist, there are only 80 degrees of rotation.

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Twist
trickipedia.description: A Cheat 720 twist is a twisting technique. You jump and set like a 540 and complete 2 rotations before landing. You land on the leg that you pump up initially on take-off. 1/2 of the rotation is started on the ground hence the Cheat 720 name. also commonly known as 540 Twist. Your 540s and Butterfly Twists should be strong (and on the same side) before attempting to train this move.

trickipedia.name: Shuriken Corkscrew Feilong
trickipedia.description: A Shslugken Corkscrew Feilong is a Corkscrew variation where you would execute a Shslugken Corkscrew and complete the technique with a Frontkick or Inner Crescent. You would land on the Shslugken leg. prerequisite_ids: Shslugken Corkscrew and Feilong.

trickipedia.name: Pop 720 Triple
trickipedia.description: A Pop 720 Triple is a 'pop' trick in which the person executes a Pop 360 Kick completing the first 360 degrees of rotation. Followed by an Inner Crescent or Roundkick. Which is then followed by a Hook, completing the second 360 degree rotation. Same target area should be hit all 3 times. prerequisite_ids: a strong Pop 360 and pop 720 Double.

trickipedia.name: Singleleg Hook
trickipedia.description: A Singleleg Hook is a Singleleg with a hook kick prior to landing. Comparable to a 720 Double, but inverting. Normally left alone as a single trick. Prerequisite: A strong Singleleg (must be able to complete Singleleg high at the peak of jump to give yourself enough time to execute hook kick).

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg Hook
trickipedia.description: A Doubleleg Hook Kick is a a Doubleleg with a Hook Kick prior to landing. Normally left alone as a single trick. prerequisite_ids: A strong Doubleleg (must be able to complete doubleleg high at the peak of jump to give yourself enough time to execute Hook Kick).

trickipedia.name: Swipeknife
trickipedia.description: A Swipeknife is a Sideswipe with a Hook Kick prior to landing. A Swipeknife is to a Sideswipe as a Jacknife is to a 540. Practitioner should have a sideswipe efficient enough to complete with enough height and rotation to execute the Hook Kick. Prerequisite: Sideswipe and Jacknife

trickipedia.name: Paraknife
trickipedia.description: A Paraknife is a Parafuso AKA Doubleleg540 with a Hook Kick prior to landing. A Paraknife is to a Parafuso as a Jacknife is to a 540. prerequisite_ids: Parafuso. Practitioner should also be efficient with C720 Hook Kicks and Jacknives.

trickipedia.name: Boxcutter
trickipedia.description: A boxcutter is a hypered corkscrew with a hook kick prior to landing. A boxcutter is to a cork as a hyperhook is to a butterfly twist. Commonly executed from a j-step set up, but also comboed from the raiz or the butterfly twist. Practitioners should train hyper corkscrews until efficient enough to look over shoulders and execute the hook kick.

trickipedia.name: Hyperhook
trickipedia.description: A Hyperhook is a Hyper Butterfly Twist with a Hook Kick prior to landing. This technique can be left alone as a single trick, but is also a strong candidate for combos. Can be used before or after other tricks in combo. prerequisite_ids: Hyper Butterfly Twist

trickipedia.name: Hyperswipe
trickipedia.description: A Hyperswipe is a technique where the tricker would Aerial, rotate 360 degrees, and execute a defined Sideswipe on the way down before landing. Normally left alone as a single trick. prerequisite_ids: Aerial Twist and Sideswipe.

trickipedia.name: Webster X-out
trickipedia.description: A Webster Xout is a swingthru Frontflip jumping off one leg, and executing an xout while upside-down. prerequisite_ids: Webster and Xout or Splitkick.

trickipedia.name: Crowd Awakener Level 2
trickipedia.description: A Crowd Awakener Level 2 is a Corkscrew with an inverted Split Kick (leaning back) at the peak of the Corkscrew. Hips should be facing up towards the sky at the point of lock-out. The Split Kick should also be comprised of Inverted Roundkicks or Twistkicks. prerequisite_ids: Corkscrew and Split Kick.

trickipedia.name: Half X-out Half
trickipedia.description: This trick essentially combines a full twist and an X-out, where the X-out takes place after the first 180° rotation, and followed by the second 180° rotation.

trickipedia.name: Hyper Gainer Full
trickipedia.description: A Hyper Gainer full is a Fulltwisting Gainer landing on the hyper leg. In this case you would land on the same leg that you swing up initially upon take-off. Much like a hypered Corkscrew, but person rotates on a vertical axis, where hips go significantly higher than the head. Commonly executed after a Raiz or a Butterfly Twist. prerequisite_ids: Gainerfulltwist

trickipedia.name: Shaolin Cross
trickipedia.description: This move is a swigthrough into a hyper feilong where you treat the act of swinging through as if it were a kick.

trickipedia.name: Doubleleg X-out Swipe
trickipedia.description: A Doubleleg Xout Swipe is a Bio Flip with an Xout (split) at the peak of the flip and a defined Sideswipe on the way down. prerequisite_ids: Doubleleg Xout (AKA Kim Do Kick) and Sideswipe.

trickipedia.name: Quintuple Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A Quintuple Flashkick is a flipping technique with 5 complete Flashkicks executed in the air prior to landing. Normally performed after a roundoff. prerequisite_ids: Flashkick, Double Flashkick, Triple Flashkick, Quadruple Flashkick

trickipedia.name: Butterfly twist Swipeknife
trickipedia.description: A Jacknife'd Hyperhook.\r\n\r\nA Butterfly Twist Round with a Hook Kick prior to landing. Must be able to go from Butterfly Twist Roundkick right into the Hook Kick with good extension, but not breaking the flow of rotation.

trickipedia.name: Full Swipe
trickipedia.description: A Standing Fulltwist with a defined Swipeswipe executed at the peak of rotation. Must be able to finish Fulltwist on the way up in order to execute Sideswipe on the way down with enough room.

trickipedia.name: Cork Swipeknife
trickipedia.description: A Corkscrew variation where practitioner would execute a Corkscrew Roundkick and follow with a Hook Kick prior to landing. Must be able to finish Corkscrew Roundkick with enough height to complete the Hook Kick.

trickipedia.name: X-Out Full
trickipedia.description: An X-Out with a Fulltwist to finish. Should leave arms open dslugng X-out to allow yourself to wrap for the Fulltwist.

trickipedia.name: Gainer Arabian X-out
trickipedia.description: A Gainer with a halftwist or rotation, and an Xout while upside-down.\r\n

trickipedia.name: Grandmaster Twist
trickipedia.description: A combination of the Grandmaster Swipe and the Aerial Twist.  The person would perform a Grandmaster Swipe (an Aerial where the first leg misses the ground) and when inverted a half twist is performed before landing.

trickipedia.name: Quadruple Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A Quadruple Flashkick is a flipping technique with 4 complete Flashkicks executed in the air prior to landing. Normally performed after a roundoff. prerequisite_ids: Flashkick, Double Flashkick, Triple Flashkick

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Twist Hyper
trickipedia.description: The performer takes off of one foot, executes 540 degrees of rotation in the direction of the takeoff leg (ie if you take off of your left leg then you spin to the left), and then lands on the non takeoff leg (if you take off your left leg and spin to your left then you land on your right leg.).

trickipedia.name: Cheat 720 Twist Round
trickipedia.description:
A Cheat 720 twist round is a twisting and kicking technique. 

You jump and set like a 540 and complete 540 degrees of rotation before landing (It is 540 and not 720 because when you take off you are looking at the sky, and when you land you look at the floor). You land on the leg that you pump up initially on take-off and throw kick with the non landing leg. 

1/2 of the rotation is started on the ground hence the Cheat 720 name. also commonly known as 540 Twist. 

A strong ch720 twist is essential for this move.

trickipedia.name: Grandmaster Swipeknife
trickipedia.description: A Grand Masterswipe followed by a hook kick before landing.

trickipedia.name: Flash Full
trickipedia.description: A flash kick follwed by a full twist.  The kick which is normally kicked straight out would now be kicked out and then around like a crescent kick which will initiate the twist.  This is usually performed from a round off but can also be done as a gainer.

trickipedia.name: Pop 720 Hyper
trickipedia.description: Performer takes off 2 feet, spins 540 degrees, then throws an outside crescent or hook kick. The performer must land on the kicking leg.

trickipedia.name: Crowd Awakener Level 3
trickipedia.description: The performer,from a cheat setup, does a 360 split kick followed by a hook kick with the leading leg.

trickipedia.name: Kroc
