trickipedia.description: A corkscrew that spins the opposite way. If the performer swings their right leg up, they twist to the right in a Kroc as opposed to the left in a cork.

trickipedia.name: Pop Swipe Knife
trickipedia.description: A pop swipe that is hooked after the swipe is executed.\r\n\r\nThis trick is swipe knife that is executed from the pop setup

trickipedia.name: Dragonfly Corkscrew
trickipedia.description:
A corkscrew round with the round kick happening before the twist.

The performer uses the swinging leg to execute a defined round kick, followed by a twist to finish the trick. Significantly harder to perform than a regular corkscrew.

It can be viewed as a swingthru into a snapuswipe.

trickipedia.name: Backside 1080 Jacknife
trickipedia.description: A backside setup into a 1080 Jacknife

trickipedia.name: Backside 1260
trickipedia.description: a backside set-up into a pop 1260 kick.

trickipedia.name: Holy Hook
trickipedia.description: The opposite to a moonkick as the hook in the move is on the opposite side to the takeoff leg.\r\n\r\nThe practitioner performs a gainer switch with a hook kick executed with the non-landing leg.

trickipedia.name: Full-Up
trickipedia.description: A fulltwisting flip where the full is executed before the flip.

trickipedia.name: Vertical Wall Run
trickipedia.description: A wall run in parkour is a technique where a practitioner runs toward a wall at an angle, using momentum to propel themselves upward and forward along the wall's surface for a few steps. This move allows them to scale obstacles, reach higher ledges, or transition to other movements like jumps or flips. It requires precise timing, speed, and coordination to execute effectively.

trickipedia.name: Gate Vault
trickipedia.description: The Gate Vault is a vault used to lower oneself over a fence, gate or wall, allowing one to quickly move over the obstacle and retain most of their forward momentum. Think of it as a Parkour Roll, but right after your roll starts you fall to the side in a cartwheel.

step_by_step_guide.title: Run up to object
Place one hand (e.g. the left hand, with the palm up) on the wall, rail or gate.

tips: Sometimes you may want/need to not put your stomach on the obstacle. It's a little more challenging.
step_by_step_guide.title: Lean over object resting your stomach on the obstacle
Pop over the rail and arch the back, bringing the feet over the head
step_by_step_guide.description: Similar to a front handspring, cartwheel, or round-off.

step_by_step_guide.title: Place your feet on the ground and remove your hand

trickipedia.name: Drop In
trickipedia.description: Dropping in from the top to your back.

trickipedia.name: Roundoff
trickipedia.description: Roundoff

trickipedia.name: Speed Vault
trickipedia.description:
The Speed Vault is a vault used in Parkour. A Traceur or Traceuses will use this vault to quickly overpass an obstacle, such as a low wall, railing or barrier, while preserving forward momentum for additional movements such as a roll or underbar. It is used frequently in all types of urban environments.

It is performed by: - Running up to the obstacle. - Pushing off of one foot. - Placing one hand on the obstacle (if you jumped off of your right foot use your right hand and vice versa). - Swinging both legs over the obstacle to one side. - Landing on the ground and continuing running.

It can be turned into a safety vault, the most basic vault in parkour, by placing the outside leg on the obstacle as you pass (to maintain height) and then swinging the inside leg through. It can also be turned into a Two-handed vault by placing the outside hand next to the inside hand on the obstacle.

step_by_step_guide.title: Run toward the obstacle
Launch off of the leg on the same side as the hand placed on the obstacle,
Brush hand on object just enough to launch yourself forward over the obstacle,
Come out in one-footed run, landing on the leg you used to launch over.

trickipedia.name: Lache
trickipedia.description: The Lache is parkour or freerunning move used to swing off of a bar or branch. After swinging the traceur can grab onto another bar or branch, or land in a precision, crane or cat.

step_by_step_guide.description: Hang on a bar or a branch.

Try to swing forward and backward by moving your legs forward and backward
Once you're swinging, gain momentum by going backward
Swing forward and when reaching the most forward point, throw yourself off the bar with your hands

Now, if you want to land on a second bar, pop your hips up, extend your arms to grab it and continue swinging. If you just want to land in any other place, use the landing technique you prefer, such as a crane, a cat, etc.

trickipedia.name: Tic tac
trickipedia.description: Two steps on the wall horizontally

trickipedia.name: Monkey Vault
trickipedia.description:
The Monkey Vault is a vault used in parkour which involves planting two hands side-by-side on an obstacle and bringing both legs in-between, shortly after pushing off with the hands. When the hands are placed on the object before the feet leave the ground, it is known as a monkey vault, however if the feet leave the ground before the hands reach the object, it is referred to as a kong vault.

The vault is designed to mimick the way a monkey walks, with their hands on the outside and then bringing their feet in-between their arms.

step_by_step_guide.title: Run towards the obstacle
Place hands down on the obstacle
Push off the obstacle while jumping
Bring legs into a tucked position to clear obstacle
Land safely

trickipedia.name: Front Flip
trickipedia.description: One complete forward rotation

trickipedia.name: Swivel Hips
trickipedia.description: Seat drop half turn to seat drop.

trickipedia.name: Round-Off
trickipedia.description: A cartwheel variation landing with both feet together, facing backward. Provides a powerful entry for back tumbling passes.

trickipedia.name: Back Extension Roll
trickipedia.description: A backward roll to a handstand, pushing up to stand. Combines rolling and handstand strength.

trickipedia.name: Stomach Cruise Stomach
trickipedia.description: Stomach drop half turn to stomach drop

trickipedia.name: Wall Flair
trickipedia.description: A flair movement performed on the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Aerial
trickipedia.description: A no-hands cartwheel, rotating sideways with momentum. Requires strong leg drive and core control.

trickipedia.name: Stomach Drop
trickipedia.description: Drop to your stomach

trickipedia.name: Swan Dive
trickipedia.description: Starting from your feet you do almost a full rotation but end up on your back.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Switch
trickipedia.description: A butterfly kick in which the performer switches legs in order to land on the second leg that leaves the ground.

trickipedia.name: Back Handspring
trickipedia.description: A backwards rotation, where the person plants both hands on the ground and "springs" off on to his feet.

trickipedia.name: Front Handspring
trickipedia.description: A forward leap onto hands, pushing off with shoulders to land on feet. Introduces forward flipping with hand support.

trickipedia.name: Knee Drop
trickipedia.description: Drop to your knees

trickipedia.name: Tornado Kick
trickipedia.description: The Tornado Kick is one of the most basic kicking tricks. It is a prerequisite to numerous kicking tricks that come with higher levels of tricking.\r\n\r\nFrom a ready stance, the performer turns away from the target as if to perform an outside spin kick...instead, that action is faked, and the knee pulls upwards sharply as the performer jumps off his base leg. At this moment, a round kick is delivered and the performer lands on the non kicking leg to finish the move.

trickipedia.name: Reverse Aerial
trickipedia.description: Where the practitioner runs backwards into an aerial. The leading leg swings into the aerial without touching the ground.

trickipedia.name: 180 Cat Leap/Euro Step
trickipedia.description: Merge a Cat Leap with a Tic Tac and voila! You have a 180 Cat Leap. Useful for where you want to be is behind where you're doing the Cat Leap to, doing Cat to Cat, or for a nice turn-around, landing back where you started.

step_by_step_guide.title: Do a Cat Leap
step_by_step_guide.description: oh yea, if you haven't done a Cat Leap before, don't do this and go practice those first

step_by_step_guide.title: Drive one of your legs up, using that force to push the same foot into the wall, kicking you off with a spin
Land where you want to, whether that be on something or in another cat
step_by_step_guide.description: you did it!

trickipedia.name: Turn Vault
trickipedia.description: Useful for where a Gate Vault wouldn't work, or where you want to get into a Cat Leap postion to climb down an obstacle. The vault involves going up to a obstacle, putting your hands on in a switch-grip (one hand gripping inside one hand gripping outside), jumping your hips and feet over the obstacle, rotating on the hand that has it's knuckles pointed away from you, grabbing, and ending in a Cat Leap postion.

step_by_step_guide.description: Approach the obstacle as if you were to perform a basic side vault
Once you begin the vault, one hand and arm will remain stationary, the arm with the underhand grip, while the other arm will turn. The arm that turns has the overhand grip
As you rotate 180 degrees to the other side of the obstacle, you want to finish as if you just landed a cat leap (legs and knees bent arms above your head)
Dismount the obstacle, then land appropriately (such as the safety tap or Parkour roll)

There are a number of ways to come out of a turn vault. One is to simply drop to the surface below. Launching out to another obstacle (cat to cat) or away from the object that you turn vaulted, continuing left or right along the outside of the obstacle, or even pop vaulting back towards the direction whence you came are all options as well.

Learning

trickipedia.name: Cradle Revert
trickipedia.description: A cradle position return movement on the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Backwards Roll
trickipedia.description: It's the parkour roll done backwards. Mastering it can prevent a lot of injuries when falling backwards.
trickipedia.tips_and_tricks:
-Can be learned easily by just doing a forward parkour roll and then directly reverting that movement.
-When falling backwards from a standing position, look towards the horizon, this makes sure that your chin travels towards your chest naturally without the danger of head to ground whiplash effect, that can occur from pressing that down too stiff or not engaging the neck at all.
trickipedia.common_mistakes: Your head shouldn't touch the ground
trickipedia.safety_notes: Master it on soft ground first

trickipedia.name: Butterfly kick
trickipedia.description: Butterfly kick involves dipping down, jumping off one leg while the other swings back, and then in the air when you are horizontal, scissor your legs before landing

trickipedia.name: Masterswipe
trickipedia.description: A cartwheel performed off of the other/wrong foot.

trickipedia.name: Fred Astaire
trickipedia.description: A fun twisting trick

trickipedia.name: Swan Dive
trickipedia.description: 3/4 of a frontflip off of the face of the wall

trickipedia.name: Dive Roll
trickipedia.description: Jumping into a roll. This is not a gymnastics roll. You must go over your shoulder.

trickipedia.name: Handstand Cut Through Drop In
trickipedia.description: A handstand position on the wall transitioning to a drop in with a cut through motion.

trickipedia.name: Cradle
trickipedia.description: A back drop with a half twist to another back drop

trickipedia.name: Swingthru 360 Hyper
trickipedia.description: A swing hyper wheel kick.

trickipedia.name: Full Turn
trickipedia.description: Doing a 360 on the face of the wall while planting your feet.

trickipedia.name: Lazy Vault
trickipedia.description: The Lazy Vault is a vault used for moving over an obstacle sideways or diagonally while still running or moving forwards. It is also sometimes confused with a Thief Vault; however, the thief vault is a hybrid vault consisting of elements of the Dash Vault and the lazy vault.

step_by_step_guide.description: Run parallel or diagonally to the obstacle
Place inside hand on an object and force inside leg over the object
Push off with outside leg while swinging an inside leg over the object in an arc
Follow inside the leg's arc with outside leg while letting go of inside hand and touching the ground with the inside leg
Touchdown with outside leg while pushing off of object with outside hand
Run out with split feet

trickipedia.name: Back Tuck
trickipedia.description: A backward somersault with knees tucked, landing on feet. A key skill for back tumbling passes.

trickipedia.name: Macaco
trickipedia.description: The macaco is a trick that resembles an off axis, one handed back handspring or backflip that is usually initiated from a squat. It is commonly used as a transitioning move or a progression towards back handsprings or backflips.
trickipedia.tips_and_tricks: The distance that someone can kong mount an obstacle is usually a very similar distance as the proper distance for a macaco, so a macaco can serve as a stylish backwards dismount after a kong mount.
trickipedia.safety_notes: Turn your head to the side to spot your landing

trickipedia.name: Pullover to top
trickipedia.description: A pullover movement from the trampoline bed to reach the top of the wall.

trickipedia.name: Illusion Kick
trickipedia.description: Similar to an aerial except the front leg (the one that came up last) is pulled over to create an axe like effect. A illusion kick is to a illusion twist as a b-kick is to a b-twist.

trickipedia.name: Horizontal Wall Run
trickipedia.description: it's just a harder tic-tac, this can be done with 3-5 steps (though 5 is hard)
trickipedia.safety_notes: if doing it over a drop always roll if you fall

trickipedia.name: Front Half
trickipedia.description: A front flip with a half twist

trickipedia.name: Back tuck
trickipedia.description: A single backward rotation

trickipedia.name: Kickback
trickipedia.description: A pullover motion to touch the wall followed by a backflip or gainer off the wall.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist
trickipedia.description: A trick in which a 360° twist is being executed while the body rotates 180° through the transverse plane (a flatspin). This trick is executed from the 'butterfly' setup, and the wrap for the twist occurs at the same time you would kick up the back leg in the Butterfly Kick.

trickipedia.name: Dash Vault
trickipedia.description: The Dash Vault is a vault used in Parkour and Free running in which a practitioner jumps over an obstacle feet first then leads the body away from the object with the hands. This vault is considered a more difficult vault to perform because of its difficulty to transfer momentum.

step_by_step_guide.title: Run up to the obstacle
Jump off of one leg kicking the other leg up
Lean back

tips: A little push can give you a boost
step_by_step_guide.title: Place your hands on the obstacle
Land safely back in a stride

trickipedia.name: 360 Front Kick
trickipedia.description: The 360 front kick is essentially a tornado kick in which you swap the inside crescent for a front kick. This kick is slightly more difficult than a standard tornado kick because you have to make it appear that you have stopped your momentum while you execute the kick. Your momentum won't actually stop, but rather your stance will open up and the momentum will continue to travel through your lead/landing leg.

trickipedia.name: Aerial Hook
trickipedia.description: An aerial where the trailing leg is pulled down like a kick instead of following through like a standard aerial.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist
trickipedia.description: A B-kick with a twist

trickipedia.name: Walking Backflip
trickipedia.description: A backflip executed while moving up the wall surface.

trickipedia.name: Arabian Dive Roll
trickipedia.description: Starting backwards you do a 180 into a roll.

trickipedia.name: 180 Re-Grab
trickipedia.description: A bar movement in which the traceur  swings forward, releases their grip on the bar, completes a half twist (180 degrees rotation), and re-grabs the same bar they swung from.

tips: If you “throw” the bar back with your release hand, it will make the rotation/twist easier
step_by_step_guide.title: Progression
step_by_step_guide.description: Release only one hand from the bar and turn away from the released hand and swing back in a mixed grip swing.

tips: As you first do this move, you probably won’t grab in the same place as you released, but as you improve, try to re-grab closer and closer to where you released.
step_by_step_guide.title: Complete Move
step_by_step_guide.description: Once you have the progression down, release both hands, but still maintain the principle of a throw hand in order to make the rotation easier.

trickipedia.name: Walking Sideflip
trickipedia.description: A sideflip performed while transitioning up the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Wall Backflip
trickipedia.description: A backflip on the wall, and be one two or three steps.

trickipedia.name: Touchdown Raiz
trickipedia.description: Initiated in the same manner as a normal Raiz however after take off, one hand will touch down to the ground before the feet land.  The hand that touches down is on the same side as the initial kicking leg.  So after the lift off the hand reaches back towards the shoulder and the back should arch allowing you to spot the ground.  Make contact first with the hand then as the lead foot touches the ground push the hand off the ground and begin lifting up the chest.

trickipedia.name: Aerial Twist
trickipedia.description: The Aerial Twist is the inverted version of the Butterfly Twist.\r\n\r\nWhen the Aerial is initiated, the arms reach forward just like in any Aerial; but once you kick the back leg and launch off the base leg, you bring the arms to the inside of the body. If you kick your right leg backwards on the Aerial, then your right arm should wrap to the left side of your body. If you kick your left leg backwards, then your left arm should wrap towards the right side of your body. When wrapping with the arms, turn your head in the same direction as the wrap, and squeeze your legs together. The trick should be landed one leg at a time.

trickipedia.name: Aerial Switch
trickipedia.description: A trick in which the base leg of an Aerial is pushed along it's course faster than the back leg, so that instead of landing on the back leg (like in a regular Aerial), you land back on the base leg.

trickipedia.name: Corner Turn
trickipedia.description: Starting from the top, twisting to your back

trickipedia.name: Cradle 540
trickipedia.description: A cradle with an extra twist

trickipedia.name: Hyper Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: A corkscrew with an extra 180 degree rotation so you land on your trailing leg.

trickipedia.name: Arabian
trickipedia.description: A trick where the performer jumps up and rotates 360° forwards along the sagittal plane (or about the x-axis) as well as rotating 180° along the transverse plane, before returning to his feet. He should land facing the opposite direction from which he took off.

trickipedia.name: Gainer
trickipedia.description: A running back flip off of one foot that gains forwards momentum

trickipedia.name: Walking Barani
trickipedia.description: A front flip with a half twist performed while transitioning up the wall.

trickipedia.name: Back Layout
trickipedia.description: A trick where the performer jumps up and rotates 360° backwards along the sagittal plane (or about the x-axis), before returning to his feet. What sets this apart from a regular backflip is the lack of a tuck. Instead of bringing the knees to the chest, the practitioner uses his back muscles to force the rotation while at the same time bringing his hips as high as possible to maintain height.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist Swipe
trickipedia.description: The performer executes a Butterfly twist where at they throw a kick with the non takeoff leg that follows the rotation of the butterfly twist. The performer then lands on this leg.

trickipedia.name: Thief Vault
trickipedia.description: The Thief Vault is a slightly modified or hybrid movement of the Lazy Vault and the Dash Vault. It is less well-known and is considered by some to not be a useful technique or even a proper movement. It is used as a slightly faster alternative to the Lazy Vault, and the exit is more perpendicular to the obstacle as in a Dash Vault, rather than being parallel to the obstacle as in a Lazy Vault.

step_by_step_guide.title: Approach the obstacle as you would in a lazy vault, with the inside arm on the obstacle.
Jump off using your inside leg, and rather than swinging it over the wall, swing your outside leg up and over before the inside leg.
Place the other hand behind you, keeping the elbow bent not straight to absorb the impact.
Push off with your hands and exit in the same way you would in a dash vault; with your legs split, and come out running (or use an appropriate landing if necessary).

trickipedia.name: Corner Pops
trickipedia.description: Running the wall and jumping off of the top using the corner.

trickipedia.name: Front Flip
trickipedia.description: A trick where the performer jumps up and rotates 360° forwards along the sagittal plane (or about the x-axis), before returning to his feet.

trickipedia.name: Corner dive return
trickipedia.description: A diving return movement from the corner of the wall.

trickipedia.name: Sideflip drop in
trickipedia.description: A sideflip used to drop in from the wall to the trampoline.

trickipedia.name: Brandy
trickipedia.description: A no-handed round-off. A front-half.\r\n\r\nThis move is similar to a barani (where the takeoff is off of both feet instead of one).

trickipedia.name: Back full
trickipedia.description: A backflip with a single twist

trickipedia.name: Kash Vault
trickipedia.description: The Kash Vault is a vault consisting of a combination of the Kong Vault and Dash Vault.  The kash vault is executed similar to a kong vault, but during the middle of the vault, the traceur swings his/her legs through their arms instead of going straight over.  This vault is considered a relatively easy vault because of its slow speed, but for beginners, this vault can be tricky.

step_by_step_guide.title: Run towards the obstacle
Launch with your legs and dive
Place hands down on the obstacle
Swing your legs through your hands and extend them in front of you instead of tucking them
Push yourself away from the obstacle

trickipedia.name: Back Handspring
trickipedia.description: A backward flip onto hands, snapping back to feet with a strong push. A cornerstone for back tumbling series.

trickipedia.name: Kong Vault
trickipedia.description:
The Kong Vault (also originally known as the Cat Pass) is a vault used in parkour for moving over an object and gaining distance. It is an advanced variation of the monkey vault. Kongs are useful for moving over long objects, such as large barriers. 

The same movement can be used to move over rails and fences. Kong Vaults are commonly used for distance and power, instead of speed. Though they take extra time to learn, they are one of the most common and should be trained early on. There are a number of variations of the Kong Vault. 

Take note that when you use the Kong Vault, you have to dive towards the obstacle the Kong Vault will be performed on such as a picnic bench.

step_by_step_guide.title: Get a running start and dive forward
Put your hands down on the obstacle
Push downward the obstacle
step_by_step_guide.description: Your goal is to push hard enough to bring your chest up so your feet can easily return to the ground.

step_by_step_guide.title: Land back on your feet.
step_by_step_guide.description: You typically want to land in a stride (one foot then the other), but you can land two feet for a precision.

trickipedia.name: Gainer
trickipedia.description: A backflip that travels forwards

trickipedia.name: Back Tuck
trickipedia.description: Backflip

trickipedia.name: Arabian crash dive drop in
trickipedia.description: An Arabian flip combined with a crash dive motion as a drop in.

trickipedia.name: Punch Front
trickipedia.description: Punch Front

trickipedia.name: Back Handspring
trickipedia.description: a gymnastics move where you jump backward from a standing position, arch your body into a handstand, and then quickly push off your hands to land on your feet

trickipedia.name: Webster
trickipedia.description: Front swing front flip

trickipedia.name: Back Layout
trickipedia.description: A backflip with a straight body

trickipedia.name: Cheat Gainer
trickipedia.description: J-step backflip with a slight slant.

trickipedia.name: Front Flip
trickipedia.description: A front flip, or front tuck, is a flip where you jump, rotate your body 360 degrees forward, and land on your feet. Key to a successful front flip is generating vertical height with a powerful jump and arm movement, then tucking the body by bringing the knees to the chest to increase rotational speed. Practicing basic forward rolls and dive rolls is crucial for building the foundational movements and coordination needed for a front flip.

trickipedia.name: Handstand-forward-roll
trickipedia.description: Handstand to forward roll

trickipedia.name: Wall Spin
trickipedia.description: A wall spin

trickipedia.name: Inward 360 Roll
trickipedia.description: While lifting one leg, like you would kick a ball, jump into the opposite direction inwards and roll out.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist Double Leg
trickipedia.description: A Twist Doubleleg is a variation of a Butterfly Twist where your legs extend and meet together like a Doubleleg540 (or Parafuso) and your body is in a pike position. You land on both feet at the same time just like a Doubleleg. prerequisite_ids: Butterfly Twist, Parafuso

trickipedia.name: Flyway
trickipedia.description: A swing into a backflip off of a bar

trickipedia.name: Narabong
trickipedia.description: A Narabong is a combination of a Tornado kick and a skip hook. It is executed form a cheat/vanish set up. From a cheat setup, the user chambers the initial lifting leg, throws a round kick with the second lifting leg, and then flings a hook kick out while landing on the round kicking leg. A Narabong is a Jacknife executed on the ground, as opposed to in the air.

trickipedia.name: Walking Jonah
trickipedia.description: A Jonah flip (front flip with hands touching) performed while walking up the wall.

trickipedia.name: Turtle
trickipedia.description: A 360 flatspin off of the wall.

trickipedia.name: Axe2Aerial
trickipedia.description: The Axe2Aerial is essentially an aerial with an axe kick as the setup/momentum-builder.

trickipedia.name: Palm Spin
trickipedia.description: Using your hands on the corner of a ledge to do an inverted 360 and land back on the same side you started from

trickipedia.name: Corkscrew Doubleleg
trickipedia.description: A Corkscrew Doubleleg is a Corkscrew where your legs meet together and your body is piked just like a Doubleleg. Most commonly executed in combo after a Raiz, or a Butterfly Twist, or a simple reverse set up. You must have strong Corkscrews and Doublelegs or Parafusos before attempting this technique.

trickipedia.name: Star Kip
trickipedia.description:
The Star Kip-up occurs when the practicioner goes from the ground back to his feet by spiraling his legs together, shifting his weight onto upon his shoulders, and pushing off the ground with his upperbody and arms.

This trick generally begins with you back facing the ground.

The design of this trick also causes the body to rotate 180° while kipping upwards, so when you land on your feet, you should be facing the opposite direction you were when you initiated the trick.

trickipedia.name: Aerial drop in
trickipedia.description: A no-handed cartwheel or aerial used to drop in from the wall.

trickipedia.name: Back Half
trickipedia.description: A back layout with a half twist, landing facing forward. Introduces twisting in back tumbling.

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Knife
trickipedia.description: A jacknife'd butterfly kick. A butterfly kick follwed by a hook kick before/while landing. A hyperhook without the twist. A butterfly knife is to a hyperhook as a moonkick is to a boxcutter.

trickipedia.name: Hyper Aerial (Mega)
trickipedia.description: The Hyper Aerial is an Aerial landed on the opposite foot by way of overrotating. As the legs travel overhead, the base leg is swung along its course faster than usual. This is done by squeezing the buttocks and making the whole body tight.\r\n\r\nHyper Aerial actually lands in Mega position, though Mega wasn't recognized when the Hyper Aerial came up.

trickipedia.name: Neck Breaker
trickipedia.description: A Neck Breaker (or Kong front ) is a parkour move combining a Kong vault by diving forward, placing hands on an obstacle, and pushing off directly into a front flip. Unlike a front handspring, this move requires pushing off the block to generate height for the rotation, rather than just driving the heels over.

trickipedia.name: Reverse Vault
trickipedia.description: A vault where you use your outside hand to do a 360 over an obstacle

trickipedia.name: Pop 3
trickipedia.description: A pop movement with three rotations on the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: 2 Step Wall Run
trickipedia.description: Wall run up a wall using 2 feet

trickipedia.name: Front Handspring
trickipedia.description: an acrobatic skill in gymnastics and parkour where a person hurdles or lunges forward, plants their hands on the ground, and then pushes off with their arms to land back on their feet

trickipedia.name: Run To Handstand
trickipedia.description: Running the wall into a handstand

trickipedia.name: Layout
trickipedia.description: A backward somersault with a straight body. Requires precise form and power.

trickipedia.name: Whip Back
trickipedia.description: A fast, low backward somersault used to connect other skills in a pass. Emphasizes speed over height.

trickipedia.name: 360 Drop In
trickipedia.description: A 360 twist from the edge of the wall dropping in to your back.

trickipedia.name: Barani drop in
trickipedia.description: A barani flip used as a drop in from the wall.

trickipedia.name: Barani
trickipedia.description: A front tuck with a half twist, landing facing backward. Combines forward rotation with twisting.

trickipedia.name: 2 Step Wall Flip
trickipedia.description: Wall flip with 2 steps

trickipedia.name: Gainer Switch
trickipedia.description: The performer sets up into a gainer, swings their leg up, takes off and begins to somersault whilst kicking their foot over their head. At the peak of the jump the performer switches the leg that leads and lands back onto the takeoff leg.

trickipedia.name: Demon
trickipedia.description: A 360 from your back using your hands to rotate you

trickipedia.name: 360 swan drop in
trickipedia.description: A 360-degree swan rotation used as a drop in from the wall.

trickipedia.name: Walking 360 swan
trickipedia.description: A 360-degree swan rotation while walking the wall.

trickipedia.name: Ring up
trickipedia.description: An upward ring motion on the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Front Half
trickipedia.description: A front layout with a half twist, landing facing forward. Complements Barani with a straighter body position.

trickipedia.name: Front Full
trickipedia.description: A front flip with a full twist

trickipedia.name: L-Sit
trickipedia.description: Catching the corner in a L position

trickipedia.name: Double Kong
trickipedia.description: Two kongs in a row before touching the ground

trickipedia.name: Butterfly Twist Round
trickipedia.description: A butterfly twist with an inside kick delivered before/while landing. Uninversion is more acceptable with btwist rounds than standard btwists.

trickipedia.name: Wall Full
trickipedia.description: A wall backflip with a full twist

trickipedia.name: Loser
trickipedia.description: A front flip where you move backwards

trickipedia.name: Gainer Full
trickipedia.description: A gainer with a full twist

trickipedia.name: Vampire
trickipedia.description: An inverted handstand on the face of the wall or the corner

trickipedia.name: Walking 1&3
trickipedia.description: A combination trick involving one and three-quarter rotations while walking the wall.

trickipedia.name: Scissorswipe (Aerial)
trickipedia.description: An Aerial Scissorswipe

trickipedia.name: Double Frontflip
trickipedia.description: An advanced acrobatic maneuver where you perform two consecutive forward rotations in the air from a single jump, landing back on your feet.

trickipedia.name: Palm flip
trickipedia.description: A flip initiated with palm contact on the wall.

trickipedia.name: Corkswipe
trickipedia.description: A Corkswipe is a technique where the tricker would Corkscrew and execute a defined Sideswipe on the way down.  Tricker should be able to perform their Corkscrew rotation fast and early in order to give room to show the Sideswipe properly, otherwise it will look like a Hyper Corkscrew. prerequisite_ids: Corkscrew and Sideswipe

trickipedia.name: Loser Flyaway
trickipedia.description: Opposite of a flyaway or a front flip but when you swing back you front flip

trickipedia.name: Toilet Bowl
trickipedia.description: Toilet Bowl

trickipedia.name: Corkscrew
trickipedia.description: J-step gainer with a 360

trickipedia.name: Pop 3 thread the needle
trickipedia.description: A pop 3 combined with a thread the needle position.

trickipedia.name: Tic Tac 360
trickipedia.description: A tic tac with a 360

trickipedia.name: Dive Front
trickipedia.description: a dive (usually from a ledge/drop) where the person does a frontflip midair, then when at the ground they roll to absorb the impact through their body

step_by_step_guide.title: steps:
step_by_step_guide.description: jump with your comfy foot and get into a dive, about 1 quarter of the way tuck and do a frontflip, once you land do a roll and continue your session!

trickipedia.name: Shant
trickipedia.description: An off axis backflip

trickipedia.name: Pop Swipe
trickipedia.description: A modified 360 face pop similar to a 540 kick

trickipedia.name: Front 1 and 3
trickipedia.description: An advanced acrobatic maneuver where you perform one and 3/4 forward rotations in the air from a single jump landing on your back.

trickipedia.name: Ballout To Top
trickipedia.description: A ballout flip that lands at the top of the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Inward Vampire
trickipedia.description: Starting from a cradle revert position, you do 3/4 of a frontflip catching into a vampire position

trickipedia.name: Back Pike
trickipedia.description: A backward somersault with straight legs and a folded body. Increases difficulty over back tuck.

trickipedia.name: Flyaway Full
trickipedia.description: A flyway with a full twist

trickipedia.name: Front Layout
trickipedia.description: A forward somersault with a straight body. Less common but highly technical.

trickipedia.name: Rudy
trickipedia.description: A front flip with 1.5 twists

trickipedia.name: Back 540
trickipedia.description: A backflip with one and half twists

trickipedia.name: Side Flip
trickipedia.description: A sideflip

trickipedia.name: Back Full
trickipedia.description: A back layout with one full twist (360°). Combines straight-body rotation with twisting.

trickipedia.name: Hero Flip
trickipedia.description: This technique lets you do a flip off a drop parallel to you by using the momentum of a swinging leg to start the rotation. Similar to a J-step its been popularized by the creator Hero DW

trickipedia.name: Walking side full
trickipedia.description: A sideflip with a full twist executed while walking up the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Aeriola
trickipedia.description: A variation of the Aerial, the Aeriola starts with a butterfly twist setup. Right before the practitioner dips for the butterfly twist, he transfers his momentum forward into an aerial.

trickipedia.name: Corkscrew
trickipedia.description:
A Corkscrew is a Btwist from a Moonkick approach.

As the base leg pivots into the Moonkick, the hands are brought back towards the hips. Once the base leg is blocked in, the trail leg kicks across and upwards (much like kicking a soccer/football). As this happens, the practitioner jumps off of his base leg and wraps his arms across his body in the same direction as he kicked his trail leg. While wrapping, the legs are brought in tightly and the head turns in the appropriate direction to aid the twist. After 360° of rotation, the trick is landed in the same way as a Btwist would be.

Like the Btwist, the Corkscrew is supposed to have flatspin of 180°.

trickipedia.name: Flash Kick
trickipedia.description: A backflip variation where you kick a foot out early and land one foot after the other

trickipedia.name: Ballout barani to top
trickipedia.description: A ballout followed by a barani flip, landing at the top of the wall.

trickipedia.name: Side Gainer Drop In
trickipedia.description: A side gainer used to drop in from the wall to the trampoline.

trickipedia.name: Back Flip
trickipedia.description: A backflip, also known as a back tuck, is a flip where you jump, rotate your body 360 degrees backward, and land on your feet. The key to a successful backflip is a powerful jump that generates significant vertical height. This is achieved by swinging your arms up and extending your legs forcefully. Immediately after leaving the ground, you must tuck your body by pulling your knees to your chest to accelerate the backward rotation. Before attempting a full backflip, it's essential to master foundational exercises like back rolls and handstand fall-overs to build body awareness and the necessary muscle memory for the movement. A progression for this is a macaco.

trickipedia.name: 360 Dive Roll
trickipedia.description: A dive roll with a full twist

trickipedia.name: Inward Side
trickipedia.description: Inward side

trickipedia.name: Double Backflip
trickipedia.description: Two backwards rotations

trickipedia.name: Cork drop in
trickipedia.description: A corkscrew rotation used to drop onto the trampoline from the wall.

trickipedia.name: 1&3 drop in
trickipedia.description: A one and three-quarter rotation drop in from the wall.

trickipedia.name: Back 540 Dive Roll
trickipedia.description: Starting backwards you do one and a half twists into a roll. This is typically done out of a round-off

trickipedia.name: Daywalker
trickipedia.description: A vampire without the twist.

trickipedia.name: Ring down
trickipedia.description: A downward ring motion on the trampoline wall.

trickipedia.name: Double Full
trickipedia.description: A flip with two full twists. In this trick, the performer jumps up and rotates 360 degrees backwards along the sagittal plane (or about the x-axis) while at the same time spinning 720 degrees along the transverse plane, before returning to his feet.

trickipedia.name: Arabian
trickipedia.description: A half twist into a back tuck or layout, landing facing forward. Blends front and back tumbling dynamics.

trickipedia.name: Front 2 and 3
trickipedia.description: Almost a triple front flip but you land on your back.

trickipedia.name: Double pullover to top
trickipedia.description: Two consecutive pullover movements to reach the top of the wall.

trickipedia.name: Double Full
trickipedia.description: A back layout with two full twists. Combines rapid twisting with straight-body control.

trickipedia.name: Double Full
trickipedia.description: A backflip with two full twists.

trickipedia.name: Rudi
trickipedia.description: A front tuck or layout with 1.5 twists, landing backward. A complex forward-twisting skill.

trickipedia.name: Front Double Full
trickipedia.description: A front flip with two twists

trickipedia.name: Walking back full
trickipedia.description: A backflip with a full twist performed while moving up the wall.

trickipedia.name: Double Layout

loopkicks.name: POP 360 SHURIKEN
loopkicks.description: The Pop 360 Hyper starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with an outside cresecent kick towards the target and lands in complete stance. This move is often the first Shuriken that trickers learn, and is vitally important for future shuriken variations.

loopkicks.name: SKIP HOOK
loopkicks.description: The skip hook is an important, fundamental move to master. All vert kicks of higher rotation that end in hook or any hyperhook variations all have the same muscle-memory as a skip hook. How your skip hook looks will determine how all your other hook kicks will look like, so master it until it looks exactly how you want it to look.

loopkicks.name: POP TORNADO
loopkicks.description: The most basic of pop kicks. The Pop Tornado starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with a round kick towards the target.

loopkicks.name: BACKSIDE 720
loopkicks.description: The Backside 720 starts in Backside stance, rotates 180° in the air, then finishes with a hook kick. Could be thought of as a Skip Hook off of two feet.

loopkicks.name: POP FEILONG
loopkicks.description: The Pop Feilong starts off like a Pop 360 and finishes with an inside kick.

loopkicks.name: TORNADO
loopkicks.description: The Tornado is the most basic cheat kick and one of the first moves a tricker will learn. It forms the basis of all cheat kicks after.

loopkicks.name: POP 360
loopkicks.description: The Pop 360 starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with an outside crescent kick towards the target and lands in turbo (both feet). This trick is also a hyper, because Pop 180 Hook (TKT) is not often used in tricking. This trick is vitally important for Illusion Twist and other similar tricks that end in turbo.

loopkicks.name: SWING 360
loopkicks.description: A turbo’d skip hook basically, or a Pop 360 from a swing.

loopkicks.name: CHEAT DOUBLE HOOK

loopkicks.name: TORNADO SIDEKICK

loopkicks.name: POP 720

loopkicks.name: CHEAT 720
loopkicks.description: A cheat vert kick that takes off in (roughly) backside stance, spins 360° and finishes with an outside (hook or crescent) at the target.

loopkicks.name: CHEAT FEILONG
loopkicks.description: A Tornado where the outside (pumped, non-kicking) leg kicks like a crescent, resulting in 2 kicks.

loopkicks.name: BACKSIDE 900 SIDEKICK

loopkicks.name: CROWD AWAKENER

A Tornado that lands in hyper (extra 180°) on the inside kicking leg.

loopkicks.name: BACKSIDE 900 FEILONG

loopkicks.name: BACKSIDE 900
loopkicks.description: The Backside 900 starts in Backside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 540° in the air, then lands and finishes with a round kick towards the target. The Backside 900 is very important for learning similar tricks like the Cheat 900 and the Swing 900, as they have the same amount of rotation, but different take off.

loopkicks.description: The most basic of pop kicks. The Pop Tornado starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with a round kick towards the target.

loopkicks.name: Pop 360
loopkicks.description: The Pop 360 starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with an outside crescent kick towards the target and lands in turbo (both feet). This trick is also a hyper, because Pop 180 Hook (TKT) is not often used in tricking. This trick is vitally important for Illusion Twist and other similar tricks that end in turbo.

loopkicks.name: Pop 360 Shuriken
loopkicks.description: The Pop 360 Hyper starts in Frontside stance, leaves the ground off of both feet, rotates 180° in the air, then lands and finishes with an outside cresecent kick towards the target and lands in complete stance. This move is often the first Shuriken that trickers learn, and is vitally important for future shuriken variations.

loopkicks.name: Backside 900

The Backside 900 is very important for learning similar tricks like the Cheat 900 and the Swing 900, as they have the same amount of rotation, but different take off.

loopkicks.name: Skip Hook
loopkicks.description: The skip hook is an important, fundamental move to master. All vert kicks of higher rotation that end in hook or any hyperhook variations all have the same muscle-memory as a skip hook. How your skip hook looks will determine how all your other hook kicks will look like, so master it until it looks exactly how you want it to look.

loopkicks.name: KAYDEM
